Chapter 1
Foreword

Foreword / Safety notes
1 Foreword
1.1 Safety notes
It is important that you observe the listed safety precautions.
Signal words - definitions
Danger
Indicates that there is exceptionally great danger when handling the respective product.
If a warning is not heeded and no precaution against danger is taken, there will definitely
be a serious injury or even death.
Warning
Identifies a possible danger of a product, which can lead to serious injuries or even
death if adequate precautions are not taken.
Caution
Indicates a potentially dangerous situation that can lead to moderate or minor physical
injuries or to material damage.
Note - Material damage
Indicates a potentially dangerous situation. If it is not prevented, the product or
something near it could be damaged.

Note - Material damage
Indicates a potentially dangerous situation. If it is not prevented, the product or
something near it could be damaged.
2 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Foreword / Safety notes
The safety notes listed here relate to the risk assessment performed in accordance with
the Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC concerning potential residual risks that may exist
despite all possible technical protection measures being performed.
Warning symbols if used on components and their meaning:
Danger - Danger to life from electric shock
Indicates life-threatening situations due to electric current. Failure to follow the safety
instructions may result in serious injury or death. The work being performed may only be
carried out by a qualified electrician who has been trained on the system.
Caution – Heating of housing surface / Avoid material damage!
The RTVision.t V4 Sensor is installed in the robot cell. If a break occurs in the safety

The RTVision.t V4 Sensor is installed in the robot cell. If a break occurs in the safety
circuit of the robot cell during regular production operation, this may give rise to
unforeseen continuous operation of the sensor lighting. This also causes a significant
and permanent increase in the heating of the sensor housing, especially at a higher
ambient temperature. The temperature of the sensor may also increase in operation
modes with a long inspection time and simultaneously short shutdown time. To protect
the hardware, the sensor temperature is monitored internally by the software and
switched off if exceeded. The unintended continuous operation of the sensor is ended
after five minutes by the internal safety cutoff.

switched off if exceeded. The unintended continuous operation of the sensor is ended
after five minutes by the internal safety cutoff.
This time frame can be changed in consultation with appropriately trained and
authorized personnel. Should it be necessary to dismantle the sensor, switch it off
separately first (including the strobe controller), check that it is not live and proceed
using suitable personnel protection equipment (PSA).
Responsibility of the operator
The system is used in the commercial and industrial sector and integrated in an existing
system. The operator is therefore bound by legal obligations to comply with standards
and work safety regulations and to observe the safety, accident prevention and

and work safety regulations and to observe the safety, accident prevention and
environmental regulations valid for the area of application of the system. Claims of any
kind due to damage resulting from improper use are excluded and the operator alone is
liable. Furthermore, the operator is responsible for intercepting signals issued by the
system as envisioned and to integrate these signals in his system control and process
them. The image processing system is parameterized in accordance with the quality
assurance thresholds prescribed by the customer/operator. Subsequent changes to
these tolerances are not the responsibility of the service partner.
Operating manual RTVision.t 3
V. 4.8

Foreword / Safety notes
Precautionary measures during use
Danger of electric shock.
High voltage - Danger of electric shock.
Some of the components operate under high voltage. Disconnect the system from the
power supply for cleaning and maintenance. In particular, always switch off the strobe
controller before carrying out any work on the sensor. Always make sure that there is no
voltage present before starting work.
Observe the supply voltage
Use the device only with the specified voltage.
Live cables
Do not damage, modify, over-bend, twist or pull the power cable. Do not place any
objects on it and keep it away from heat (insulation could melt).
Damaged cables must be replaced immediately by trained staff .
Warning - Danger of electric shock and fire

Damaged cables must be replaced immediately by trained staff .
Warning - Danger of electric shock and fire
Workplace - Danger of electric shock and fire
Do not use the device at locations with high humidity, high levels of dust or oily
smoke/vapors.
Abnormal conditions - Danger of electric shock and fire
If smoke or noise develops, disconnect the device from the power supply immediately
and contact the authorized service partner.
Foreign bodies/liquids - Danger of electric shock and fire
Never insert foreign bodies into the plug, slits or other openings. Never place drinks or
other liquids on or directly beside the device.
If foreign bodies or liquids get into the device, switch off the power supply immediately
and contact the authorized service partner.

If foreign bodies or liquids get into the device, switch off the power supply immediately
and contact the authorized service partner.
Dropping/toppling and damage - Danger of electric shock and fire
If the device should topple over, be dropped or damaged, switch off the power supply
immediately and contact the authorized service partner.
You must not reach into the RTVision.t system - Danger of electric shock and fire

Foreword / Safety notes
Additional components may only be installed by trained and authorized personnel and

Dismantling - Danger of electric shock and fire
Never dismantle or modify the device. To extend the hardware, contact the supporting
service partner.
Danger - Electric shock during maintenance
Must be observed before and during system maintenance
· All maintenance work may only performed by qualified specialists. Observe the
accident prevention regulations of the employers' accident insurance fund relating to
work on electrical equipment in this respect and the safety precautions described
here.
· The system must be fully de-energized before the control cabinet is opened. Please

here.
· The system must be fully de-energized before the control cabinet is opened. Please
see the electrical wiring diagram for details of disconnecting from the mains supply.
Before disconnecting the power supply, close the application as described above
and shut down the Vision Controller PC properly.
· The complete system must be disconnected from the power / voltage supply before
working on the sensor (replacing the protective screen, screwing on/unscrewing).
This especially applies to the strobe controller. Please see the electrical wiring
diagram for details of disconnecting from the mains supply. Before disconnecting
the power supply, close the application and shut down the computer.
· Before opening the computer housing ensure that the mains plug is pulled out and all

· Before opening the computer housing ensure that the mains plug is pulled out and all
cables to external devices have been removed.
· When changing computer components ensure that this does not release any static
discharges.
· Never open the computer power unit. It does not contain components that require
maintenance. System components may still be carrying life-endangering voltages
even after the mains plug has been pulled out. If the power supply unit is damaged,
have it replaced by an authorized expert.

Foreword / Safety notes
Caution
If a device is not used for a prolonged period, disconnect it from the mains.
If you replace or renew live cables, remember that the maximum cable length is 28 m or
30m.
At least once per year:
Remove the plug from the socket and remove the dust around the pins of the plug.
Caution - Observe the light source
Do not look directly into the LED light source!
Obey your sensitive eye reflex or natural aversion reaction and do not look into the
sensor lighting.
Continuous or high frequency lighting can give rise to stroboscopic effects, which similar
to shadowing, flickering or flashing can trigger epileptic seizures in at-risk persons.
Note – Material damage
Operation of the system

to shadowing, flickering or flashing can trigger epileptic seizures in at-risk persons.
Note – Material damage
Operation of the system
· You should not make changes to parameters unless you have received instruction in
the meaning of the individual parameters.
· If you have made changes to parameters, do not forget to save or accept them.
· If you want to switch off the system, do not simply turn off the computer. First close
the application properly and then shut the computer down.
· When using high image frequencies, the software takes up considerable system
resources. The system is designed accordingly. However, additional programs
and/or tasks can interfere with the inspection and teaching-in process and should

and/or tasks can interfere with the inspection and teaching-in process and should
therefore be avoided at all costs. If you have to run other software in parallel with
RTVision.t then contact the manufacturer beforehand.
· Back up the system regularly, as the content of the memory could be lost or changed
due to a malfunction or repair of the device. To restart the system, you need at least
the folders Appl and Ini in order to ensure a workable system.
· Do not subject the system to any excessive electrical or magnetic fields that could be
caused by electro-welding, for example.
· When operating the RTVision.t system, comply with the requirements regarding the
ambient temperature. Do not subject the system to excessive heat or cold. Ensure

ambient temperature. Do not subject the system to excessive heat or cold. Ensure
that there is always sufficient fresh airflow for the computer and control cabinet.
6 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Foreword / Safety notes
· Do not operate the system in a contaminated or dirty environment, or in damp or
heavily particle-laden air conditions.
· The device is suitable and has been tested for a power supply of 230V at 50Hz.
Manual setting is therefore not necessary. Remember that the maximum cable length
is 30m.
· The RTVision.tsystem is developed by default for use on the Windows 10 / 64 bit
operating system.
Transport
· The RTVision.t system (control cabinet) must never be moved when it is switched on.
· Use the original box / packaging material for transport or shipment.
Intended use

The RTVision.t system is used to inspect and document glue beads with digital imaging
methods for errors, interruptions or deviations. To do this, before starting operation, the
parameters for detecting errors must be defined in the application software of the
RTVision.t system in accordance with the customer's specifications.
· The product is state of the art and is safe to operate.
· If the product is not used properly or not used as intended, this could result in a
dangerous situation.
· Unauthorized modifications and/or changes to the product are not permitted for
safety reasons.
· The RTVision.t system may only be installed, operated and maintained by trained
and authorized specialist personnel.
· No permanent workplace is intended on the system with this product. The product

and authorized specialist personnel.
· No permanent workplace is intended on the system with this product. The product
can generally be operated in automatic/normal operation without permanent
supervision.
Operating manual RTVision.t 7
V. 4.8

Foreword / Safety notes
ESD information
Electronic assemblies with integrated circuits are very sensitive to excess voltages and
electrostatic discharges.
Caution - Material damage
Electrostatically sensitive assemblies can be destroyed by discharges below the human
perception threshold. These discharges occur, for example, by touching an assembly or
its connections. The damage may also only occur after a longer period of operation.
Basic precautions to protect against electrostatic discharges:
· Ensure good grounding when handling assemblies (e.g. during maintenance,
replacing components, cleaning and similar work)
· Avoid touching contacts or electric components
· If measurements have to be carried out on assemblies, those carrying out the task

· Avoid touching contacts or electric components
· If measurements have to be carried out on assemblies, those carrying out the task
must ground themselves beforehand, e.g. by touching a grounded metal surface or
by wearing suitable ESD-compatible shoes.
· Make sure to always observe the occupational safety regulations at your workplace.

Intended use
The RTVision.t system is used to inspect and document glue beads with digital imaging
methods for errors, interruptions or deviations. The system can use image sequence
recordings to capture the entire glue bead and then inspect and assess it in sections inline
during the application of glue when attached to the robot arm in accordance with the
parameterization. To do this, before starting operation, the parameters for detecting errors
must be defined in the application software of the RTVision.t system in accordance with the
operator's specifications. Also note that the RTVision.tsensor has been designed for a
defined area in terms of working distance and calibrated accordingly ex works. Deviations

defined area in terms of working distance and calibrated accordingly ex works. Deviations
from the boundary conditions over and above the prescribed framework (see data sheet) may
lead to a decrease in performance as well as the accuracy of result data and process
precision. RTVision.t is not a measurement technology system, rather can be classified
under visual inspection systems. Tolerances are compared and evaluated using image

Chapter 2
Introduction

Introduction / Short description – Technical requirements
2 Introduction
2.1 Short description – Technical requirements
The bonding or sealing of components as a replacement or complement for other joining
techniques is commonplace in a broad range of industries. Both methods can be used to join
a wide variety of materials, often with the help of robots. Despite cutting-edge application
technologies for glues and sealants, it is often not possible to keep process parameters
constant. Since bonds and seals require high quality, automatic process control is required.
The inline glue and sealant application inspection system RTVision.t delivers a high quality
inspection. The user sets all the parameters once and then the inline inspection system

inspection. The user sets all the parameters once and then the inline inspection system
performs a fully automatic visual inspection of the glue or sealant application directly during
the actual application process.
The .t stands for "TRACKING"
Note
Unless otherwise noted in this manual, the term "glue" can also be replaced with
"sealant".
The system inspects the following characteristics:
· glue bead width
· glue bead continuity
· glue bead position relative to the applicator nozzle
· glue bead position relative to a component edge (reference, option of parameterization)
· glue bead distance from a component edge (double reference, option of
parameterization)
Hence, the system contributes to ensuring the rigidity, solidity, leakproofness of the joint and

parameterization)
Hence, the system contributes to ensuring the rigidity, solidity, leakproofness of the joint and
ensures a defined crash behavior. Performing the inspection during the actual application
process avoids additional inspection times and bulky inspection setups.
Note
The term "CONTROLLER" is used in this manual to mean the robot controller or the
feed controller (adhesive application controller).
The image processing system RTVision.t communicates with the robot controller and/or feed
controller, but it is always the subordinate system.

Introduction / Short description – Technical requirements
RTVision.t must be able to communicate with the robot controller for the purpose of
exchanging various signals and data.
The signals are used, for example, to transmit:
• Inspection results
• Start / stop events or
• General system messages and data
Communications is via fieldbus systems (e.g. ProfiNET, ProfiBUS, Ethernet IP, CANopen,…)

the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take
place in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following

Introduction / Short description – Technical requirements
Technical requirements
The potential applications of the inspection system are dependent on the shape, color and
surface structure of the component, the type of glue application, properties of the glue as well
as the guiding of the nozzle during the application of the glue.
A detailed feasibility study therefore has to be performed based on original components and
glues prior to configuration of the inspection system.
The sensor, which is positioned vertically with respect to the glue bead, should ideally be
parallel to the surface of the component while the glue is being applied. Slight tilting of the
sensor is possible (max. +/- 8°), but not usually practicable owing to the dependency on the

sensor is possible (max. +/- 8°), but not usually practicable owing to the dependency on the
component surface, the component geometry and the condition of the glue. Depending on the
sensor model, a predefined working distance must be maintained over the entire inspection
section.
Owing to external influences (e.g. strong light changes, different metal sheets, degree of oil
contamination or basic contaminants), deviations can arise in practice in the image
processing system. The light conditions in the robot cell must therefore no longer change
significantly following the initial configuration of the system, as they could otherwise falsify the
image recording.
The RTVision.t system is an inline inspection system, which performs the image recording

image recording.
The RTVision.t system is an inline inspection system, which performs the image recording
directly after the application of the glue, which means that the robot should be moved on
approx. 2- 3 cm after the glue application nozzle is switched off in order to also record the
end of the inspection track. This area can be parameterized separately in RTVision.t so as to
allow the complete inspection track to be checked.
Operating system
The RTVision.t system is developed by default for use on the Windows 10 / 64 bit operating
system.

Unfortunately, we cannot accept liability for any errors that may occur in this manual and for
any operating errors in the inspection system that may result from this.
We appreciate your understanding in this respect.
Launching the online help / structure of the online help
The online help is accessed via the F1 key. The help is available in both automatic mode and

Launching the online help / structure of the online help
The online help is accessed via the F1 key. The help is available in both automatic mode and
configuration mode. The online help is opened as an HTML manual and contains no contextsensitive help.
The best way to find what you are looking for is via Search and List Topics or Display. In
this way, you discover more about the term you are querying in different contexts.
If you follow the links in the individual help topics, click Back to return to the previous start
pages.
In addition to explaining the individual functions, this manual also provides a description of the
basic functions and sequences in the section "Quick start" to get you up and running with our

basic functions and sequences in the section "Quick start" to get you up and running with our
product. Frequently used terms in the help are explained briefly in the glossary.
Navigation
Navigation buttons are provided in the top right area of the online help. Use these buttons to
open the different pages of the manual. They are used to call up the previous and following
help topic as well as to return to the start page. The navigation bar is always visible at the top
right-hand side of the window.

Chapter 3
Function overview

Function overview / Function overview
3 Function overview
3.1 Function overview
New in RTVision.t V.4.8:
· The number of main modules has been reduced to RTVision.t server RTVision.t client
RTVision.t viewer
· The functions of RTVision.t export have been integrated in the server.
· The statistics area has been extended to include trend indicators using arrow symbols
(parameterizable) - the layout of the simple statistics has been changed.
· Image sequences can be clearly identified from the path.
· Image and especially error sequences can be loaded globally at any time for
reparameterization. This means that more than one faulty inspection can be analyzed.
· The glue definition area has been simplified in relation to parameterization.

· The glue definition area has been simplified in relation to parameterization.
· The number of sampling points per system can be changed dynamically, the default value
has been increased compared with the previous version - the result from curves has
been significantly improved.
· The definition of default parameter sets and the associated specific renaming of areas is
now possible.
· Global settings for areas, references and polygons at product, inspection and area level
can be implemented.
· A distance check from the edge of the component to the center of the glue has been
added.
· Optional database usage with PostgreSQL is possible (special specification).
· Ghost mode for checking communication during commissioning (special specification).

· Ghost mode for checking communication during commissioning (special specification).
· The language setting (special specification) can be made centrally.
· Full screen mode can be set as opposed to window mode (special specification).
· The software interface is defined by two different color styles (special specification).
Note
The use of special specifications always requires prior consultation with the
authorized service partner, as many of the special specifications can only be
activated under license or with due regard for customer specifications.
The actual parameterization of the software is performed in configuration mode (client). This
involves setting all the parameter values required to inspect the components in automatic
mode (server).

involves setting all the parameter values required to inspect the components in automatic
mode (server).
The application is transmitted to the server at the end of the parameterization and only then
enters the test process.

Function overview / Function overview
An application can contain several products / components that are selectively called from the
higher-level controller via specific IDs.
This means that the actual glue inspection is carried out in automatic mode. The glue bead is
checked in the current image sequence in this case and the visual results are compared with
the specifications and their tolerances.
The results calculated inline are displayed as OK, NOK or WARN on the server interface
screen during the check by the server.
A new feature is the display of trends when several defects are detected one after the other at
the same location. Depending on the selected default setting, the direction of the arrow
changes depending on the set number of components.

the same location. Depending on the selected default setting, the direction of the arrow
changes depending on the set number of components.
The RTVision.t viewer is used to visualize inspection results and show them in specially
prepared graphics - older data series that already exist can likewise be imported from xml
report files.

Function overview / Module views
3.2 Module views
RTVision.t consists of three standard modules:
RTVision.t Server - Automatic mode
RTVision.t Client - Configuration mode
RTVision.t Viewer
The RTVision.t interface was revised visually and structurally with this software version.
With a modern look and feel and restructuring of the function buttons including adaptation to
the UI of the RTVision.t family, the interface now offers greater transparency and clarity for
working with RTVision.t.
The Viewer screen layout is very similar, if not directly comparable, to the server. The only
way to view more information in the viewer is through the detailed view.
RTVision.t also offers the possibility to choose between two differently colored interfaces.

RTVision.t also offers the possibility to choose between two differently colored interfaces.
The light variant, familiar over many years and also predominantly used here in
documentation for reasons of better (paper) readability.
The dark variant is often used in production for viewing.

Function overview / Automatic mode (Server)
3.3 Automatic mode (Server)
The actual workpiece inspections are performed in Automatic mode . This involves

inspecting the glue bead in the current image and comparing the results with the
specifications. The application file created in configuration mode is used as a basis for the
inspection. The name of the current inspection file is displayed together with the storage path
in the status bar in automatic mode.
After the image processing software launches, the system is in automatic mode and is ready
to perform inspections provided parameterization has been successfully completed.
The following overview shows the basic inspection sequence:

V. 4.8

Function overview / Configuration mode (Client)
3.4 Configuration mode (Client)
The actual parameterization of the software is performed in configuration mode . All

parameters and their tolerances required to inspect the workpieces in automatic mode are
preset here.
Configuration mode is explained in greater detail in Configuration mode description . You

will find details of how to switch to the client in Change between server and client .

The following overview shows the basic parameterization sequence: The application file
currently open is displayed by name together with its storage path in the status bar of the
client window.
It must be completed and then transferred to automatic mode (server) for further processing.

Function overview / Starting and exiting the application
3.5 Starting and exiting the application
The application normally starts automatically when the Vision control computer is turned on. If
a manual start is required, start the program by double-clicking the <%PRODUCT_NAME%>
(server) icon on the Windows desktop (you will need to be logged in as a Windows
administrator for this).
The application always starts in automatic mode and is ready to perform inspections once
loading is complete.
The program can only be exited after entering a password. This must be set up
via user management or by the administrator.
RTVision.t (server) and client communicate with each other.
If both programs are open and the connection data (IP) is correct, select Connect to
connect the client and server.

If both programs are open and the connection data (IP) is correct, select Connect to
connect the client and server.
If both programs are connected, the corresponding button in the client is highlighted in green
.
If the application is in automatic mode, the RTVision.t (server) checks the product(s) using a
parameterized application.
If the software is to be shut down properly, e.g. for maintenance or repairs, select Exit
program on the RTVision.t (server).
Approval by user management is followed by closure of the RTVision.t (server).
The client can be opened via Unlock configuration mode.
Also select Exit program there and after the password-protected process has been
confirmed, the RTVision.t client is also closed.
If required, you can now shut down the computer.

confirmed, the RTVision.t client is also closed.

the cables, switch off the strobe controller (not via the software, but directly in the
control cabinet). Make sure that the system is disconnected from the mains
before performing any work on the sensor or in the control cabinet.

Function overview / Switching between automatic mode and configuration mode
3.6 Switching between automatic mode and configuration mode
To do this, change from RTVision.t (server), which performs an inspection in automatic
mode, to the RTVision.t (Client), in which you can specify the precise definition of the
application in configuration mode.
You can optimize the parameters in configuration mode without interrupting the
inspection currently being performed by RTVision.t in automatic mode. To do
this, you need to switch from automatic (server) to configuration mode (Client).

Function overview / Application structure
3.8 Application structure
An application basically contains the following parameter sets:
· Products
· Inspections
· Areas
· Contours / polygons
· References
· Exposure control
The hierarchy of the parameter sets looks as follows:
You will find further information on this in:
Directory structure / files

32 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Function overview / Application structure
Application file
The application file combines all the data and parameters required for the inspection.
Products
Products are the first parameter group beneath the application file. A product basically
corresponds to a workpiece type. Each application must therefore contain at least one
product. If a station processes different workpiece types, they can be created in the form of
several products within one application.
Example: Product_01 = front left door; Product_02 = front right door.
Two products were used in the illustration on the previous page. Each product is assigned a
product ID (1- 255) that can be used by the higher-level controller to select the product.

product ID (1- 255) that can be used by the higher-level controller to select the product.
The component images used for the error map in automatic mode are also stored in the
products. The glue bead can then be created in a subsequent step.
You will find further information on this in:
Product overview

Inspections
One or more inspections are created within a product. An inspection is normally created for
each glue bead applied to the component. Each product therefore contains at least one
inspection. In the example above, the first product contains only one inspection while the
second product contains two inspections.
More than one inspection will be required if the glue bead applied to a component is not
continuous.
There is no need to create additional inspections when using different tolerances for
inspecting specific areas within a glue bead. Inspections themselves can be divided into
defined, freely selectable areas. References can also be created within an inspection.
Any number of inspections can be created, however they are always subordinate to a

Any number of inspections can be created, however they are always subordinate to a
product. They are therefore managed within a product. Inspections are automatically saved
when the associated product or the application is saved.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about inspections

Operating manual RTVision.t 33
V. 4.8

Function overview / Application structure
Areas
Areas are created within an inspection. An area contains, among other things, the error
tolerances and the image processing parameters required to locate the glue bead in the
image. At least one area must be created per inspection, although there is no upper limit.
If different tolerances are to be used within a glue bead inspection, this can be achieved with
any number of areas.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about areas

Single references
References can be created within an inspection, however they are not absolutely necessary.
References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the nozzle at
specific points. This is done by setting references at these points. Any number of references
can be created within an inspection. Well-contrasted cut edges on the component can be
used as a feature for references. The basic condition is that the feature that serves as a
reference cannot be altered.
Error and warning limits are also created for references. A reference containing errors will
lead directly to an overall result of NOK.
Double references and help reference
Double references can be created within an inspection, however they are not absolutely
necessary.

Double references and help reference
Double references can be created within an inspection, however they are not absolutely
necessary.
They consist of a primary reference and an additional reference and are used to determine
the distance from the glue bead to another edge at specific points. This is done by setting
double references at these points. Any number of double references can be created within an
inspection. Any edges (e.g. component edges) can be used for inspection purposes.
Error and warning limits are also created for double references. A double reference
containing errors will lead directly to an overall result of NOK.
Furthermore, a so-called help reference can be set for the additional reference. It is required if

Furthermore, a so-called help reference can be set for the additional reference. It is required if
the distance to the center of the glue in relation to the component edge set as a reference is
to be checked at defined points of the glue bead.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about references

34 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Function overview / Application structure
Synchronization references / special mode
Synchronization references can be created at the start of an inspection before an initial
inspection – e.g. in an area – is performed. They increase the repetition accuracy of the
inspections but are not absolutely necessary for an inspection. They are based on support
points before and after the position. These support points are also taught-in images.
Any reference at the start of an inspection before an initial inspection and defined in the
direction of travel can be used as a synchronization reference. It is used to synchronize the
position of the inspection system and the robot, which permits a more precise evaluation of
the glue bead. A synchronization reference cannot be activated with Quick teach.

the glue bead. A synchronization reference cannot be activated with Quick teach.
The quality of the evaluation may be significantly impaired if a synchronization
reference is not set up correctly. It is therefore necessary to ensure that the general
conditions are adhered to when setting up synchronization references.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about references

Exposure control
Exposure control is necessary if workpieces made from different materials (e.g. Usibor,
galvanized sheet metal) with different reflective properties are to be inspected. This makes it
possible to control the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an
inspection.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about exposures

Contours
The contours are used when the camera can no longer follow the robot guidance using the
sampling points, e.g. when curves with narrow radii are to be inspected.
Polygons are then set as contours at these positions, which can be recognized and assigned
as contours by the system during the inspection.
You will find further information on this in:
General information about contours

Function overview / Image structure / image sequences
3.9 Image structure / image sequences
Camera arrangement
The diagram below shows the arrangement of the camera images around the glue applicator
nozzle. The red (dashed) line shows the switching threshold from one camera to the next.
Fig.: Schematic arrangement of the cameras in the RTVision.t V4 sensor
Images
The sensor contains three cameras arranged at 120° to each other to provide the 360°
degree view of the glue applicator nozzle required for the inline inspection of glue or sealant
application. The images recorded by these cameras are simultaneously (PC) transferred to
the vision controller (PC) over a Gigabit Ethernet interface at a frequency of 200 Hz
(equivalent to 1 image every 5 milliseconds).

the vision controller (PC) over a Gigabit Ethernet interface at a frequency of 200 Hz
(equivalent to 1 image every 5 milliseconds).
At least one of the cameras is always behind the applicator nozzle to record images with
glue.
Fig.: All three cameras replicate in individual planes or are imaged as a 360° round view
36 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Function overview / Image structure / image sequences
It can be seen in the diagram above that the camera images overlap. This is necessary to
ensure reliable recording of the glue bead at all times.
Image sequences
The high image frequency results in a large number of images that are managed as image
sequences in the image processing software. An image sequence can be considered a kind
of film consisting of a large number of individual images. The path / time information for each
individual image in a sequence is saved in an additional XML file for each sequence.
Operating manual RTVision.t 37
V. 4.8

Function overview / Inspecting the glue bead – Methodology
3.10 Inspecting the glue bead – Methodology
The methodology for glue bead detection can be configured in many ways to achieve
optimum results.
Edge detection:
For RTVision.t a glue bead essentially consists of a pair of edges. The transition from light to
dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. When searching
for a glue bead, therefore, the system is actually searching for two edges with inverse light /
dark transitions.
During the search, all the edges that are detected are evaluated via rating functions with

certain probabilities. The glue bead is formed by the two edges with the highest probability
rating.
Brightness analysis:
Under certain circumstances it may happen that the sealant is applied in such a way that the
inspection system cannot detect the edges.
As an alternative to edge detection, an option exists to perform a gray level-based brightness
analysis in designated areas. This makes it possible to inspect glue beads even without
detecting edges. This checking method is useful for checking the continuity of the applied
glue bead in places where there are no discernible bead edges (e.g. narrow housing flange or
step seams).
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the

step seams).
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the
component surface (i.e. the brightness analysis alone is not suitable for "black on
black").
In such an area, the glue bead is only defined using the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to
check the width and position of a glue bead. For that reason, the inspection is limited to a
continuity check.
Glue inspection:
The actual glue inspection is carried out within a configurable opening angle (from the glue
nozzle) and within a limited selectable search radius.

4 Configuration mode description
4.1 General information about configuration mode (Client)
In configuration mode of the RTVision.t client, all products / components are parameterized
for quality inspection on the basis of tolerances and reference characteristics. An application
file is created, which can also contain several products and is transferred to the server after
successful parameterization.
The product overview in configuration mode (Client) gives you a quick overview of the
contents of a product. It also lets you do the following:
· Create / manage products
· Create / manage inspections, including areas, references and exposures
· Display / modify the product image

image

and Help. See Menu commands in configuration mode .

the client. The application is not transmitted to the server (automatic mode) since the server

Transmits and saves the current application file loaded in configuration mode to the server in
automatic mode.
This version is saved additionally in the client.

Configuration mode description / Client screen view
4.2 Client screen view
The image processing system can be used to inspect different components (e.g. "Left door",
"Right door", "Tailgate", etc.), even if they are all processed at one station. The component
types can change in accordance with production requirements. Each component includes a
series of variables to be inspected (parameters).
Creating a new application file
An application can be created across systems during the course of commissioning by
selecting File | Local application | New on the client. The application uses the default
settings for System, Product and Inspection.
The desired products (even more than preset) can now be created within this application.

settings for System, Product and Inspection.
The desired products (even more than preset) can now be created within this application.
The default creation of products is important to initiate the storage of image recordings from
the server side.
When creating a new application, the system template comes from the server. The server
remembers which products (by means of the ID) or inspections were selected by the robot
BEFORE parameterization. Communication with the robot controller must work for this
purpose, which is why it should be applied to the components for some time before
parameterization. The server collects this data and then uses it for further parameterization.
Tip - Recording image sequences

parameterization. The server collects this data and then uses it for further parameterization.
Tip - Recording image sequences
The application file, products and inspections are created on the client, while the storage
of the image sequence(s) required for parameterization is initiated in the server module.
It is recommended to record images during all inspections. It is only if parameterization
is not possible that recordings should be made here selectively based on inspections.
See Server - Recording image sequences

calibration. These settings can be changed via the General button or tab
specifically for the selected inspection.
See General tab and Inspection length
73 75
This makes it possible to control and selectively change the exposure time of the
cameras and therefore the brightness within an inspection.
See General and Settings for exposures
76 79
Areas are used to divide an inspection into any number of sections. This may be
necessary, for example, due to different glue bead widths or changed light
conditions. The error tolerance of an area should not be set too large so that
deviations are detected in good time.
See General 80 and Settings 96 for areas

deviations are detected in good time.
See General 80 and Settings 96 for areas
References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the
glue bead at a defined position. Like areas, references must be created and
taught in.
See General 115 and Settings 117 for references
Contour detection can be used when normal glue sampling is not possible (e.g. in
curves with narrow radii). Every glue can be detected if the edges are
recognizable in the image. In this case, sampling points are not the only checking
method used, rather an entire search area is defined to search for the edges of
the glue.
See Contour detection 132
The parameterization is fully based on the image recordings obtained during
application. These image sequences can now be loaded globally.

The parameterization is fully based on the image recordings obtained during
application. These image sequences can now be loaded globally.

Note - Ensure targeted use - Material damage possible
This function should not be used lightly for any product, since it slows down the
process and puts additional load on the strobe controller.
The production startup takes longer and also the interval to the start of inspection is
increased, so that the overall system performance could deteriorate if the
parameterization is poor.

Configuration mode description / Product overview
4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths
The bead paths can be created in the product image with start and end point as well as
important intermediate points. This error map is shown with the current glue bead for testing
in automatic mode, thus allowing any possible errors to become evident during testing along
with their position. You can insert the product image and bead path prior to parameterization
and create the glue bead. The bead path can only be linked with the image sequence,
however, following parameterization of the entire inspection to enable inline consideration in
automatic mode in sync with the glue application.
Defining the bead path in the product image

automatic mode in sync with the glue application.

1. Select the product in the product list to which you want to assign the product image. By
clicking Load, the product image (different file formats such as *.jpg, *.bmp or *.png are
permitted) is loaded and displayed via Explorer.
Load Opens the data folder under the technology data, from which a specific
product image is then selected.
Save The currently displayed image can be saved separately; for this
purpose Windows Explorer opens in the technology data area.
Clear The currently displayed image is removed from the display. It remains
stored in its source directory.
2. Click Inspections to switch to the inspections list or open this list by double-clicking the
product.

Configuration mode description / Product overview
Linking the bead path with the image sequence
The bead path definition only tells the software the position of the bead in the product image.
However, it does not yet know where along the bead (e.g. at 105 mm) to mark an error. That
is why the bead path must be linked to the actual image data recorded.
The start and end point of the inspection track must be linked here in particular. To do this,
link the respective position in the product image with the position from the image sequence
(values are taken from the parameterized areas).
Tip - Linking the image sequence to the product image
Do not set up the link until the areas have been parameterized and taught-in. The

Tip - Linking the image sequence to the product image
Do not set up the link until the areas have been parameterized and taught-in. The
successfully calculated inspection length is also helpful for successful linking.
If the images for the relevant bead are no longer available in the memory, you can load the
image sequence belonging to the inspection manually.
1. From the areas of the inspection, you can use the Start and Test buttons below the area
to visualize the points in the image sequence.

Manual check
The manual check greatly simplifies the parameterization of the areas. It can be used to
determine distances and angles (to the nozzle) directly in the individual images in the image
sequence.
To be able to use the auxiliary tool manually, an image sequence must be loaded and
a calibration must be active.
In the displayed image, you can perform manual checks in all three camera images. This
gives you the option to determine point and distance coordinates.

gives you the option to determine point and distance coordinates.

clicking and recording the end point with the mouse button pressed.
The result is shown beneath Manual check to the right next to the camera images either in
table form or as a profile.
This result is ideally suited for determining the width or brightness of the glue, for example, at
every position. However, distances between the glue bead and the edge of the component
etc. can also be defined quickly. Left-clicking in the image sequence opens a context menu
for further actions (creating areas, references).

4.4.5 Run options
The settings selected in the run options are used for the run each time you navigate through
an image sequence using the image navigator.
Control elements for run options
Operation mode
The operation mode defines the settings used to run the image sequence. The following
options are available:
Test run The glue definitions stored in the individual areas are applied to the image
sequence. The system verifies whether these definitions will permit correct
detection of the edges of the glue bead. The glue bead, nozzle position and the
travel angle are depicted graphically in the image sequence in green, red and
blue. See also:
Visualization in RTVision.t 85

travel angle are depicted graphically in the image sequence in green, red and
blue. See also:
Visualization in RTVision.t 85
Teach-in run The glue bead found is taught in when this option is selected. The display in the
image navigator changes. Only the run options "Stop", "Forwards one image"
and "Play image sequence" are available. Reversing is not possible during the
teach-in run.
Optimization The optimization run is effective only in areas where the brightness analysis 91
has been activated. It updates and optimizes the previously taught-in brightness
run
70 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
values in the search window. Only the run options "Stop", "Forwards one image"
and "Play image sequence" are available. Reversing is not possible during the
optimization run!
Negative run This test run stops when the glue is found and does not stop if it is not. It is used
for negative control of the glue application and is executed without a prior order. It
is usually executed to complete the parameterization.
TIP: The inspection run without glue application is more helpful for
optimization; this is also referred to as the negative run. An image
sequence of the bead beforehand when the nozzle was closed (no
glue) is checked in this case. The correct parameterization can
therefore be checked with the inspection run and all deselected

glue) is checked in this case. The correct parameterization can
therefore be checked with the inspection run and all deselected
stop options. If the results are red, the parameterization was
successful, since the correct statement that no "glue was found"
is confirmed as an error.
Inspection run The glue definitions stored in the individual areas are applied to the image
sequence. In addition, the calculated values are compared with the taught-in
values as a way of verifying the stored error definitions. The inspection run is
therefore a simulation of an inspection performed in automatic mode. Use the
activation / deactivation options of the different stop options for this purpose.
After the operation mode is changed, playback automatically starts from the beginning of the
image sequence.

After the operation mode is changed, playback automatically starts from the beginning of the
image sequence.
Display options
Reflection filter Shows the reflection filter set in the current area or reference but also for the
contour in the camera images during the run.
The reflection filter is preset by the system when Automatic is selected.
Switching angle Shows the switching angle calculated during calibration in the camera images
(light blue dashed lines).
Stop options
These checkboxes let you automatically interrupt image playback during test / teach-in and
inspection runs. In this way you can be sure that you are not overlooking any errors.
The checkboxes have the following meanings:
Error message Image playback stops if an error occurs in accordance with the tolerance values

The checkboxes have the following meanings:
Error message Image playback stops if an error occurs in accordance with the tolerance values
set in the current area.
Warning message Image playback stops if a warning occurs in accordance with the tolerance
values set in the current area.
Single image Image playback stops if no glue is found in an image. When the standard
error number of sampling points are used, glue must not be found in any of these five
sampling points.
Single check Stops image playback at each reference or contour.
You can resume playback at any time after it stops using the image navigator.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.6.2 Inspection length
The inspection length is set in order to detect a bead path that is too long or too short.
The output is in [ms] as standard, but can also be specified in [mm] depending on the
specific customer interface definition. The time- or path-based methodology must be
stated from the outset, and must therefore be agreed in advance with the
manufacturer. Any subsequent change should be avoided as this would result in
extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot controller and in the
image processing system.
The time required to teach in an image sequence is recorded for each inspection. The teachin process should therefore only be performed when all areas have been created and

parameterized for the complete bead. A value is entered for the inspection length at the end of
the teach-in process, which includes the full length of the glue bead. A system error
(ERROR) will be output if the length of an inspection differs significantly from the length of the
taught-in inspection.
The maximum permitted deviation from the taught-in length can be specified by means of a
parameter in a system file.
This is taken into account as a Fixed tolerance in the length of the inspection.
Values in the system file are system values that may only be changed in consultation
with an authorized service engineer. To do this, the application must be closed and
reopened, which is possible only during a break in production.

with an authorized service engineer. To do this, the application must be closed and
reopened, which is possible only during a break in production.
Only the first inspection with an incorrect length is indicated, even if there are multiple
incorrect inspections. This is because if a system error (ERROR) occurs, the rest of
the bead is not checked.
An error message is output in automatic mode. This is displayed in the error information
window. Only one error message is ever written. If the length of an inspection was identified
as incorrect, the entire inspection is indicated in red in the component image.
You will find further information on this in:
Specific interface extension for path-based image processing as a special specification

4.4.7 Exposures
4.4.7.1 General information about exposures
Exposure control is necessary if workpieces made from different materials (e.g. Usibor,
galvanized sheet metal) with different exposure durations are to be inspected. This makes it
possible to control the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an
inspection.
The exposure can also be changed or optimized within the inspection in a specific area of the
inspection, since both can be interlinked by means of an identical start position.
By default, the setting is specified in [ms], but can also be specified in [mm]
depending on the specific customer interface definition.The time- or path-based

depending on the specific customer interface definition.The time- or path-based
methodology must be stated from the outset, and must therefore be agreed in
advance with the manufacturer. Any subsequent change should be avoided as this
would result in extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot
controller and in the image processing system.

clicking the vertical Exposures tab on the left-hand side of the screen.

of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.

4.4.8 Areas
4.4.8.1 General
Areas are used to divide an inspection into any number of sections. This may be necessary,
for example, due to different glue bead widths or changed light conditions. The error tolerance
of an area should not be set too large so that deviations are detected in good time.
Types of areas
A distinction is made between two different types of areas in RTVision.t :
1. Active areas: in addition to the general Area definitions they contain all Search

parameters and Error definitions that are needed to inspect a glue bead. Multiple areas
103 108
can be created within an inspection so that different inspection parameters, such as also
varying exposures, can be set.
2. Inactive areas: are used to exclude parts of the glue bead from the inspection or to
exclude active areas completely. Inspection parameters do not need to be created for
inactive areas since the glue application is not inspected in these areas. If the inactive area
at the end of the bead is replaced by a checking method, a contour can be used at this point
if necessary.
Open the Areas tab
You access the areas by switching to an inspection and clicking the vertical Areas tab on the
left in the picture.

if necessary.

4.4.8.2 Create areas

For easier detection, glue bead detection is split into areas.
Each inspection can work with several areas. These include gaps in the glue seam / glue

Each inspection can work with several areas. These include gaps in the glue seam / glue
bead as well as different widths of sections along the bead.
Properties can thus be configured individually to suit the circumstances.

dialog.

ignores individually parameterized areas and overwrites all error definitions.

properly detected (edge pair detection).

4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection
The graphic displays shown during the inline inspection of the glue have the following
meaning:
Blue circle segments Show the actual search area. The search for glue is restricted to

number of sampling points used. The default value is five and can
also be set to a higher value across systems, though there is an
upward limit.
If necessary, contact your service partner. Independent
changes in system files lead to exclusion of liability.

respective circle segment. Without the green circle, they visually

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection
A search window is positioned in the camera image over the point where the glue bead
should be. The system will only search for the glue bead within this window. The information
about the position of the inspection track is acquired from the previous image or at the start of
the bead from the user's specifications (see section Search parameters ).

Each camera has a search angle of 120° available before the camera image changes to the
next camera. The search range is always within the 120° of a camera.
This approach is possible since the high image frequency means that there are very few
differences between two successive images. Since RTVision.t inspects the entire area
around the nozzle, the actual search area is shaped like a circle segment (see dark blue
circle segments with the individual sampling lines).
Fig.: Detection of the glue bead (five sampling points)
For RTVision.t a glue bead essentially consists of a pair of edges. The transition from light to
dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. Therefore, when

dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. Therefore, when
searching for a glue bead / seam, the system is actually searching for two edges with inverse
light / dark transitions.
During the search, all the edges that are detected are evaluated via rating functions with

certain probabilities.
The glue bead is formed by the two edges with the highest probability rating.
The width and position of the glue bead is then measured.
Operating manual RTVision.t 89
V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
Tip
The default values for the glue definition can be used very effectively during standard
checks for locating the glue bead. It is only if deviations arise because of shadowing or
reflections that the default values for edge contrast or glue brightness should be defined
more narrowly. The aim always is to keep the green circle on the glue bead. The error
messages are not triggered by the search parameters, rather via the defined error
definition values. The error definition is checked by the inspection run performed after
the test and teach-in run.
If the message "Glue not found" is issued, although glue exists, then the values for the
search parameters have generally been defined too restrictively. Make sure in particular

search parameters have generally been defined too restrictively. Make sure in particular
with edge contrast and glue width not to align too strictly with the values determined in
the test run, since any values calculated only reflect the mean values for both edges,
with no separate consideration of the left or right edge.
This is performed five times (standard) per image using five sampling points. The lengths of
any interruptions in the glue bead are also recorded.
There is an option to activate a pure Brightness analysis area by area on the basis

of edge searching and detection. The glue or sealant bead in such an area is only
defined via the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to measure the width in this case. The
brightness analysis can be carried out alternatively to or also in addition to edge
detection.
The wealth of information acquired about a glue bead enables RTVision.t to rate with a high
degree of accuracy whether the glue bead is OK or not NOK. The main criteria for an OK
rating are:
· No interruptions (or a defined interruption length must not be exceeded)
· Glue bead width within predefined tolerances
· Glue bead position within predefined tolerances

4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis
Under certain circumstances it may happen that the glue is applied in such a way that the
inspection system cannot detect the edges.
As an alternative to the edge detection method described in the previous section, the option
exists to perform a gray level-based brightness analysis in designated areas. This makes it
possible to inspect glue beads even without detecting edges. This checking method is useful
for checking the continuity of the applied bead even in places where there are no discernible
bead edges (e.g. edge "merges" with component edge).
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the

bead edges (e.g. edge "merges" with component edge).
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the
component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness analysis is not suitable
for "black on black").
In such an area, the glue bead is only defined using the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to
check the width and position of glue bead here. For that reason, the inspection is limited to a
continuity check.
Detection of the glue bead by means of a brightness analysis
A brightness analysis can then be activated area by area here so that the glue bead can at
least be checked for continuity.
1. To teach in the "edgeless" area, a glue bead must first be recorded, which is covered at

least be checked for continuity.
1. To teach in the "edgeless" area, a glue bead must first be recorded, which is covered at
the problematic location with a material that contrasts with the glue in a clearly
distinguishable way.
2. These images are parameterized as before using the standard method .

5. The cover must then be removed before a second recording run.

The comparison is carried out on the glue width that is detected. If the comparison is

brightness limits can be set here and also the maximum error rate on the Error definitions
tab.
The brightness limits can be entered as relative percentages (in relation to gray level 0%
=black and 100%=white) or as absolute values.
Example of relative and absolute with an assumed average gray level of 60%:
Relative limits with +/- 20% to 60%
Absolute limits with 40% and 80% absolute to 60%
For a Detailed analysis, a Maximum error rate is entered as a % that does not relate to the
gray level % but to the number of results with a pixel-based review of the results.

gray level % but to the number of results with a pixel-based review of the results.
Example: per pixel the result is considered OK or NOK. If, for example, with 50 pixels at one
point, 11 pixels are NOK, with an error rate of 20%, the overall result is NOK.
In other words, the number of OK/NOK results per pixel is considered and this is used to
calculate an error rate. This then produces the overall result OK or NOK.
This error rate fine-tunes the evaluation even more.
Single edge detection and brightness analysis combination

In contrast to edge pair detection with brightness analysis, in this checking method the user
defines the measured width for the brightness analysis.
With single edge detection, a clear edge can be seen only on one side, the other side is, for
example, the end of the component or the glue and material have the same color and do not
create a contrast. By defining the inspection width, the inspection can be carried out
successfully in the subsequent brightness analysis.
Hence, the combination of single edge detection and brightness analysis is almost ideal in
most cases because it is the most precise checking method.

4.4.8.2.7 Glue gap
In practice, the glue definition or edge pair detection can also be used for gap variance rating.
Gap variance rating is simply inverse edge pair detection.
This means that in the basic definition, the glue appearance defines what is or is not glue.
If glue is detected, it is classified as "detected". Initially as a single image error, from a certain
length it becomes a warning / error.
The difference to edge pair detection is that here a fixed measuring start angle is defined and
does not have to be taught in.
Tip
The start angle can also be determined easily from the image sequence by using the
auxiliary tool.
Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary

auxiliary tool.
Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary
tools and right-click Create area in the context menu to open the dialog shown including the
value determined for the start angle. This can also be used to determine the start angle at the
start of a glue gap.
This angle is then valid for the whole area. If the start angle changes over time, several areas
should be used to cover the entire section.
With step seams, these gaps may not always be the same length.
So as not to generate pseudo errors, inactive areas that are as large (long) as the glue
process and signal inaccuracy should be used for the glue-gap and gap-glue transitions. To
compensate for signal inaccuracy, a synchronization reference (special mode) should be

compensate for signal inaccuracy, a synchronization reference (special mode) should be
used at the start of the inspection.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
Checking methods
Edge pair detection
The glue bead is delimited here by an edge pair. The limit values are defined by the
parameters on the Search parameters and Error definitions tabs.
By activating the Curve inspection checkbox, the system uses a different algorithm in
curves to optimize implementation of the width determination.
Edge pair detection can be further improved by marking the Additional method (Brightness
analysis) checkbox.
Single edge detection
If this checking method is selected, you can restrict the inspection to one edge of a glue bead
in the relevant area. The selection of the edge to be inspected (left or right) is dependent on
the setting under Edge transition on the Search parameters tab. Single edge detection can

the setting under Edge transition on the Search parameters tab. Single edge detection can
also be further improved by marking the Additional method (Brightness analysis)
checkbox.
The single edge detection is useful when the glue bead is close to another seam and only the
removed side forms a clear contrast with the background.
A width inspection is not possible with this setting.
Brightness analysis
This can be carried out as a stand-alone checking method or in combination with single edge
detection or edge pair detection as an additional method.
For details, refer to section Additional method (Brightness analysis) .

As an alternative to edge detection, it is also possible to carry out a pure gray level-based
brightness analysis. For details, refer to section Brightness analysis .

Gap inspection
Gap inspection allows inspection of places on the components that must contain no glue so
that they can be processed further. A typical application case is the inspection of step seams,
although this can also be covered with inactive areas and the standard methods.
In the case of active areas, but with the selective checking method Gap inspection, a NOK
message is output if glue is detected in the sampling area. For details, refer to section Glue
gap

Reflection filter
The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal.
The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be. If the reflection
filter is preset to Off, no reflections are eliminated. The reflection filter can be optionally
activated in the run options (see section Run options ). The effect is most obvious with an

activated filter in individual step mode of the currently active area. If you change the filter
value, for example, between 2 and 50, in the window above, the filter effect is interactively
visible in the displayed image.
The filter size defines the number of pixels that are grouped depending on the filter type. The
larger the value, the lower the reflection, but also the lower the edge detection.

Automatic The system automatically selects the appropriate filter type for

Open (Opening Filter) Bright interfering points on dark surfaces are removed.
Close (Closing Filter) Dark interferences on a light background are overwritten by bright
pixels.
With a high filter value (max. 50), even actual edges in the image become blurred. In

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.8.3.3 Glue model
RTVision.t uses rating functions to identify the pair of edges corresponding to the glue bead
from the multitude found in the image. These rating functions are described as the "glue

model".
The settings for the rating functions for every inspection are made in the respective area in
Area name window on the Search parameters tab.
The glue model defines the possible properties of the glue in the respective area. This is
necessary so that the software can select the correct edge pair belonging to the glue from the
multitude found in the image. For example, it defines which widths could qualify as glue.
If, for example, different application speeds result in the glue width within an area increasing
from 3 mm to 6 mm, the glue model will define the entire range between 3 mm and 6 mm as
a possible glue width.
The maximum and minimum values are shown in the Extreme values display .

4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters
The parameters or the rating functions for glue variance, glue width, edge contrast and glue
brightness for glue searches are defined on the Search parameters tab in the Area name
window.
The software needs these parameters to distinguish the glue and/or the glue edges from a
component edge, for example.
Accordingly, the rating functions differ according to the checking methodology. Thus, with
single edge detection, only the position and the contrast are to be evaluated.
In order to reliably find the glue bead from the multitude of edge pairs visible in an image, the
system first has to identify all the edge pairs in order to weight them using the abovementioned rating functions. The basic principle is described in section Inspecting the glue
bead .

The weighting takes place in the four rating functions, using four support points in each case.
The position of these support points as well as the rating at the support points can be
changed. The software calculates an overall quality rating from the four rating functions for
each edge pair. This overall quality rating can be seen in the screen area on the left in the
client during the teach-in run.
These support points can be defined with two different methods:
Simple definition with symmetrical data structure
The default includes the simple method using tolerances that are to be defined with a value in
plus/minus direction.
This definition method is the tool for unique glue quality, clear edges and symmetrical data
structure.
Operating manual RTVision.t 103
V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
In the case of symmetrical rating functions, in particular, this simplified definition can be
used especially on the basis of numeric values as a definition tool.
Variations with the same value are possible for each parameter with +/-. A value difference
between + and - does not apply here. Equally, the quality rating also cannot be influenced.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
With up to 32 parameters that can be set, this definition type is significantly more complex.
It is the ideal tool for the complex glue bead and for experts in parameter evaluation. It offers
greater scope for parameterization including quality variance.
To edit the rating functions, default values are already provided by the system, which are
displayed when creating the area.
A small interactive default setting can be made if the area was created manually with the
mouse (left mouse button) from the image sequence via the context menu Create area.
The values for contrast, width and brightness are then already preset and can be adjusted
simply to the specifications.

function cannot be determined interactively. It is also necessary to make manual corrections
here.
Another possibility to find default values when new areas are being created is to first assign
previously created default data records for each inspection. Even then, parameters must be
set depending on the test values and specifications.

V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
Glue variance rating - Find position
Rates the position of the glue. For example, the system presets the values stated below,
which must be adjusted manually depending on the test values / extreme values.
Edge contrast rating - Find contrast
Rates the edge contrast between the substrate and glue. This value cannot be determined
using the auxiliary tool. Even though a contrast is specified, if it is used then the system is
generally very restricted in its search values and tends to issue the error message "Glue not
found". If faults like shadowing or reflection then arise, delimiting the edge contrast is a
promising adjustment option that can be applied successfully with caution. 100% is the
maximum contrast. See Edge detection

Glue width rating - Find width
The width of individual edge pairs is rated here. This makes it possible to exclude edge pairs
that are too far apart. First, determine the actual width of the glue using the auxiliary tool (left
mouse button) and estimate by how much the glue width can vary. These upper and lower
limits should be given a quality rating of 1. All widths above or below this should be assigned a
quality of 0, for example, but intermediate values can also be set.
Example: The glue application should be 3 mm wide. The average width, however, is 2.5 mm
to 3.5mm. This produces the following rating function:
[mm] 2 2.5 3.5 4
Quality 0.0 1.0 1.0 0.0

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.8.3.7 Identifying and correcting parameterization errors
A test run is recommended after you have created areas. For this test run, all the options
under Stop options should be activated. This ensures that problems caused by e.g.
incorrect parameters will be identified. You should also activate the reflection filter.
Incorrect parameter settings can happen especially when areas are created manually. If this
happens, the glue bead may not be found at all or the wrong width may be determined. If all
the options under Stop options are activated, then the test run will be stopped if the glue is
not found. You can then take corrective action. Incorrectly determined widths will not cause

not found. You can then take corrective action. Incorrectly determined widths will not cause
image playback to stop. This error must be identified by the operator. The corresponding error
messages are displayed below the navigator for this purpose.
If the test run stops, you should proceed as described below. Each time you make a change,
test whether it has a positive effect on the problem and whether it might result in any negative
effects somewhere else in the glue bead.
Here is a list of the most common parameterization errors:
· The area starts in the wrong place
Check the Start position parameter in the Area name window under Basic settings.
The start position can be shifted here directly or via the Areas context menu via a
relative shift in the ms range (+/- entry possible).

The start position can be shifted here directly or via the Areas context menu via a
relative shift in the ms range (+/- entry possible).
· Playback stops directly at the start of the area
If playback stops directly at the start of the area, check whether the start angle is
correct. You will also find this information in the Area name window on the Basic
definitions tab.
· No glue found when switching from one area to the next
Check whether the Start angle is correct in the Area name window on the Basic
definitions tab. Test your changes directly in the adjacent image sequence. Use the
navigator here to follow the progress over the glue area and observe the behavior of the
sampling points in the camera view.

navigator here to follow the progress over the glue area and observe the behavior of the
sampling points in the camera view.
· No glue is found when switching from one camera to the next, the search area is not
above the glue.
Check the search area and modify it if necessary on the Search parameters tab. You
can check your changes at any time using the Test button.
· No glue is found within an area with one or more sampling points.
Check whether there are reflections on the glue bead. If this is the case, increase the
reflection filter (e.g. between 2 and 40) in the Area name window on the Basic definitions
tab.
If the glue bead merges with any seams or edges, the reflection filter setting is too high.
You can check your changes at any time using the Test button.

You can check your changes at any time using the Test button.

4.4.8.4 Teaching in areas
To teach in areas, the areas must be created successfully.
Areas are taught in as follows:
1. Switch to configuration mode and select the appropriate product / inspection.
2. Load the required image sequence.
3. Reset the extreme values display by clicking Reset.
4. With the exception of Single check, mark all the boxes on the Run options tab.
5. First start a test run by selecting Test run from the list in Run options | Operation
mode. This is the best way of finding incorrect parameter settings, since image playback
will stop immediately at these points.
6. If the test run completes without any problems, check once again whether the values

will stop immediately at these points.
6. If the test run completes without any problems, check once again whether the values
displayed in Extreme values are within the values set in the area settings in Glue
definitions.
7. If this is the case, select Teach-in run from the list under Run options | Operation
mode to start the teach-in run. Let the image sequence run until the end so that all the
areas are taught in.
