Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.9 References
4.4.9.1 General
References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the glue bead at a
defined position. Like areas, references must be created and taught in.
In addition to the existing functionality of references, double references can be used
to determine the distance between two edges, e.g. the distance between a glue bead
and a component edge to the left or right of the glue bead. They can also be used to
determine the distance from the end of a glue bead to a component edge in the
direction of travel of the glue application system (by means of a double reference).
Double references work with a primary reference and the additionally defined

reference as well as the required distance checks.
A triple reference allows the distance between the component edge and the center of
the glue to be checked with a help reference supplementing the additional reference
(edge distance of a component to the center line of the glue bead).
Synchronization references, or special mode as it is referred to, can be used to avoid
a delayed start trigger by the synchronization references acting as special references
for an inspection. This is done by using the position of a synchronization reference to
compare the calculated distance and the distance actually covered.
Opening the tab
To access the list of references, go to an inspection, double-click to open it and click the
vertical References tab on the right-hand side of the screen.

vertical References tab on the right-hand side of the screen.
Similarly, you can use the References button in the parameterization toolbar to select
these settings.
The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless
of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.
Move the mouse pointer to the camera view image that shows a reference and the glue bead.
Select the component edge and left-click to choose Create reference in the context menu.
Control elements
Add
If no reference exists as yet, the system prompts to create a new reference graphically using
the mouse.
Use the navigator to go to a suitable position in the image sequence and mark a possible
reference with the left mouse button (component edge).

Use the navigator to go to a suitable position in the image sequence and mark a possible
reference with the left mouse button (component edge).

The fully configured reference can already be taught-in after parameterization when the
reference is created. Quick teach-in is not absolutely necessary, since testing is always
carried out with the teach-in run, which takes place after the test run. If this teach-in run has
already taken place and something still needs to be corrected afterwards for a reference, the
quick teach-in phase is preferable for this single reference. The complete teach-in run of the
inspection across all elements is then no longer necessary. Special mode is an exception, as
it can only be taught in via a teach-in run.

4.4.9.2.2 Creating a reference
1. With the image navigator go to the position where the reference is to be created.

2. Mark the area where a reference is to be searched for (search area) using the mouse
(Auxiliary tools ). The direction in which you drag the search area determines the search

direction for the reference. The length of the marked area represents the search window
where the system will search for the reference edge. The start and end points of the
marking are indicated by a thin red cross and a thin green cross respectively (see picture
on left). If possible, the search area should include a light / dark edge.
Generating a reference Reference found
By default, in the error definition of the Reference name window, tolerances

are defined within which the reference must be found. The search area should
therefore be extensive enough to contain these limit values.
3. Left-click and select Create reference from the context menu.
· If the reference edge was correctly detected, click OK to accept the reference.
· If the reference edge was not correctly detected, the parameters must be adjusted.
Then confirm with Quick teach and close with OK.
A correctly detected reference will be indicated by a thick green cross (see
picture on right) following successful teach-in.

4.4.9.2.3 Primary reference
The primary reference defines the image processing parameters for a "standard" single
reference. The parameters are required to define the position of the reference in the image.
A reference basically consists of an elongated search area whose position and length are
defined by the start and end points of the search area. The system searches for edge
transitions along the search area in a similar way to when the glue bead is being inspected.
To this end, it is necessary to define whether the edge transition goes from "dark to bright" or
from "bright to dark" and, in instances where multiple edges are found, whether the first or
last edge found should be used.
Start point

from "bright to dark" and, in instances where multiple edges are found, whether the first or
last edge found should be used.
Start point
Defines the start point of the search area based on the angle and the distance to the nozzle.
These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools .

End point
Defines the end point of the search area based on an angle and the distance to the nozzle.
These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools .

Check edge variance
The box is checked if the distance between two edges is to be measured by means of a
primary and additional reference (see section Additional reference ). At the same time, an

offset (edge deviation) of the primary reference is inspected like a "normal" single reference.
This box must be unchecked if this is not to happen.

with the checkbox Distance check - Check edge variance.
If the Check edge variance checkbox is grayed out, nothing can be selected.
Edge transition
Here you can specify whether the edge transition (generally component edge) is from "dark to
bright" or from "bright to dark".
Edge position
Here you can specify whether the first or last edge found should be used for evaluation
purposes. The result of the settings is immediately visible in the adjacent image of the image
sequence.
Minimum edge contrast
Specifies the minimum edge contrast that must be achieved in order to detect an edge
transition.
Reflection filter
The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal.
The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be.

The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be.
If the reflection filter is preset to Off, no reflections are eliminated. If you change the filter value
in the above window, the filter effect becomes interactively visible in the currently displayed
image with the reference.
The filter size defines the number of pixels that are grouped depending on the filter type. The
larger the value, the lower the reflection, but also the lower the edge detection.
The pixel values can be processed with three different filter types.
Automatic The system automatically selects the appropriate filter type for
processing.
Open (Opening Filter) Bright interfering points on dark surfaces are removed.

processing.
Open (Opening Filter) Bright interfering points on dark surfaces are removed.
Close (Closing Filter) Dark interferences on a light background are overwritten by bright
pixels.
Glue appearance
For clear differentiation in relation to the gray value analysis, you have to specify whether the
glue in question is a dark glue on a bright background or a bright glue on a dark background.
The immediate effect on the image sequence can be observed in the same dialog. Since the
primary reference mostly refers only to the edge transition of the component, this setting does
not play such an important role at this point and an effect cannot be observed in the graphic
view.

matches the direction you drag the search area. The length of the marked area represents
the search window where the system will search for the reference edge. The start and end
point of the marking is indicated by a thin red cross and thin green cross respectively.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.9.2.6 Error definitions
The error definitions specify which tolerances will result in a warning or a NOK during an
inspection in automatic mode.
Warning limits, (maximum) edge deviation
If an edge is found within the search area and its position differs from the taught-in position by
more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a warning message.
Error limits (maximum) edge deviation
If an edge is found within the search area and its position differs from the taught-in position by
more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a NOK message.
Warning limits, (maximum) distance deviation
If two edges (primary and additional reference) are found within the search area(s) and their

Warning limits, (maximum) distance deviation
If two edges (primary and additional reference) are found within the search area(s) and their
distance differs from the taught-in distance by more than the value entered in this field, this
will result in a warning message.
Error limits (maximum) distance deviation
If two edges (primary and additional reference) are found within the search area(s) and their
distance differs from the taught-in distance by more than the value entered in this field, this
will result in a NOK message.
The following applies: Warning limits can only be parameterized with a lower value than error
limits.
128 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.9.2.7 Special mode / synchronization
A reference can be used as a synchronization reference. This type of reference
compensates for any positional inaccuracies caused by the delay in the trigger signal (to start
the inspection) coming from the robot. Ideally, carry out synchronization when the component
edge is easily recognizable and at a 90 ° angle to it even before the start of the glue bead.
If edge offset in a lateral direction of motion is more important, the primary or double
reference is used. In this case, however, the time delays are not compensated for,
since the relevant reference edge runs parallel to the glue bead here.
The time window of a synchronization reference must not overlap an area or a
reference.

The time window of a synchronization reference must not overlap an area or a
reference.
A reference is created in the primary reference, where the component edge is still before the
start of the first area.
The component edge must be at a 90° angle to the glue bead so that the reference search
area is identical to the running direction of the glue bead (see picture)
Now select the Special mode tab to switch to the synchronization.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
Synchronize inspection
If this box is checked, the reference can be used to synchronize the inspection. This enables
repeated inaccuracies to be compensated for.
In addition, a new teach-in phase is triggered; quick teach-in is not possible here.
Number of support points (Synchronization window)
This value indicates where the synchronization reference should be found when the selected
number of images before and after the taught-in center is considered.
The number of images in two directions creates a time/path window that must not overlap
with an active area or reference. Four support points mean, for example, images of 20 ms
before and after the centrally placed image are included and considered.

before and after the centrally placed image are included and considered.
The number of support points is limited by the robot speed and by the size of the camera
field.
In the case of the synchronization reference, the entire sequence must be taught in at the
end. Since a quick teach-in does not work here (corresponding button grayed out), the entire
inspection must be taught in via a teach-in run.
4.4.9.3 Teaching in references
References are taught in as follows:
1. Switch to configuration mode and select the appropriate product / inspection.
2. Load the required image sequence.
130 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Configuration mode description / Inspections
3. Just select Single measurement on the Run options tab in the Stop options area.

4. First start a test run by selecting Test run from the list under Run Options, Operation
mode.
This is the best way of finding incorrect parameter settings, since image playback will
stop immediately at these points.
5. Select Teach-in run from the list under Run options, Operation mode in order to start
the teach-in run. Let the image sequence run until the end so that all the references are
taught in.
The teach-in run must always be performed, otherwise the inline inspection system
will not be able to inspect any components.
You can check on the References tab in the Inspections window whether all the
references in an inspection have been correctly taught in. Failure of any of the lights
in the T. column to come on indicates that the corresponding reference was not

in the T. column to come on indicates that the corresponding reference was not
taught in. If the lamp is red, a setting is wrong. Green indicates successful teach-in;
the reference can also be confirmed (except for special mode) with Quick teach.
6. Finish by starting an inspection run to verify the teach-in run. To do this, in Run options

, Operation mode, select Inspection run from the list.

Configuration mode description / Inspections
4.4.10 Contour detection
Contour detection can be used when normal glue sampling is not possible (e.g. in curves with
narrow radii). Every glue can be detected if the edges are recognizable in the image. In this
case, sampling points are not the only checking method used, rather an entire search area is
defined to search for the edges of the glue.
To perform the relevant inspection, double-click and open the Contour name tab.
1. Select an inspection and then select the vertical Contours tab on the left-hand side of the
screen.
2. Use the Image navigator to go to the position where the polygon is to be created.

3. Click Add to create a new contour.
4. Double-clicking the contour opens the Contour name window.
5. Now click Add to add a polygon.
6. Mark this and click Edit polygon points to start the polygon editor.

Configuration mode description / Inconsistency check
4.6 Inconsistency check
If a parameter setting has been changed, an inconsistency check is performed in
configuration mode when the menu commands File | Local application | Save, File | Local
application | Save as ... and File | External application | Transfer are executed. And of
course also if the application is saved with the Save or Transmit buttons.
The inspection system performs the following checks for this inconsistency check:
· Was the sensor in the inspection(s) selected correctly?
· Was the calibration in the inspection(s) selected correctly?
· Was the selected calibration valid?
· Has the product ID already been used several times.
· Has the inspection length been taught in for all the inspections created? Is the teach-in

· Has the product ID already been used several times.
· Has the inspection length been taught in for all the inspections created? Is the teach-in
process complete?
· Have all the areas and references created been taught in?
If at least one of these checks returns a negative result, a dialog opens showing which check
was unsuccessful.
If the data is saved despite this warning, this may result in a system error (ERROR)
during the next inspection run.

Configuration mode description / Global reparameterization
4.7 Global reparameterization
This functionality allows previously parameterized elements to be reparameterized at three
different levels.
At the product level, for example, one or more relevant products can be selected and the
Settings button then activated to open a dialog that analyzes the selected products for the
number of areas, references and polygons. Similarly, inspections of a product can also be
parameterized collectively in one step.
Even at the area level, reparameterization of the areas, references and/or polygons is
possible. As soon as multiple marked list entries of areas, references or polygons are present
and Settings is selected, reparameterization is activated with Change error definitions.

and Settings is selected, reparameterization is activated with Change error definitions.
If, for example, a value for the glue gap is to be changed generally for a left and a right door,
proceed as follows:
1. Mark the products that you want to reparameterize, because they use identical or similar
parameters.
2. Open the Change error definitions dialog by selecting Settings
Fig.: For the marked product, there are eight areas (incl. inactive), three references and one created
contour
The system automatically determines the number of areas, references and polygons already
created for the selected element.
The parameters that can be set for areas, references and polygons can be changed using
the buttons.

Configuration mode description / Global reparameterization
Note - Global adoption of the new values
The system does not check for the existing values of the parameters within the
located areas, so that when a change is made, even individual limit values that are
set differently are overwritten with the new value. Thus, individual single
parameterizations are changed as well. Global reparameterization should therefore
be carried out with caution.
The core parameters that can be changed globally for areas, references and/or polygons are
as follows:
Areas References Polygons
Glue gap Edge deviation Glue gap
Glue position Distance deviation Glue position
Glue width (depends on reference type) Glue width
Checking method
Glue detection

Glue position Distance deviation Glue position
Glue width (depends on reference type) Glue width
Checking method
Glue detection
In order to retain individual settings, it is recommended to only use reparameterization in this
way at a lower level.
At the inspection level, you can specifically select the inspection for which you want to change
the applicable areas, references or any existing polygons.
A more selective approach would be possible at the area level or at a lower level with
references and polygons.

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
4.8 Sensor management
Launching sensor management in configuration mode
The required sensor must be assigned to the product in configuration mode. For this
assignment, the sensor must be created in the RTVision.t client.
Launch sensor management by selecting Manage sensors in the Settings menu in
configuration mode.
Background information:
There must be a connection to the RTVision.t server. The link to the sensor and thus
also its calibration data are saved in the sensor management on the server side.
During commissioning by the authorized service provider, this calibration data is
configured via the Sensor link checkbox. This data applies universally, but can be

configured via the Sensor link checkbox. This data applies universally, but can be
changed in the RTVision.t client per product if necessary. To enable this, assignment
of the sensor for the product is required and therefore product-specific sensor
management maintained in the RTVision.t client.
Note
Sensor calibration is carried out at the factory, saved on the sensor and generally
does not need to be replaced manually.
If you wish to perform a manual calibration, you must contact the authorized service
provider first, as the manufacturer accepts no liability for damage resulting from an
independently performed calibration.

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
4.8.1 System variants
The RTVision.t system offers different variants depending on the local installation situation,
application system and task.
Many different sensor types are available, which vary, for example, as regards lighting
parameters, filters and working distances.
The default situation for the RTVision.t system is inspection with just ONE suitable
sensor.
However, the application can be configured and set up to use several sensors. The sensor
manager of the application allows you to create several physical sensors and also virtual
sensors that differ, for example, only in their brightness. The different sensors can then be
assigned to the individual products via the RTVision.t client.

assigned to the individual products via the RTVision.t client.
If several physical sensors are in use, two conditions must be observed:
- the imaging system communicates with just ONE higher level controller
- the sensors can only carry out inspections one at a time and not simultaneously or
parallel to each other.
Within the application you can define which brightnesses are to be used for the lighting of
each sensor.
Note - Material damage
The sensor optics may only be installed and configured by the authorized service
provider. The authorized service provider works on the RTVision.t server under
Service | Hardware state and Service | Sensor settings. Leave these basic
settings on the RTVision.t server to the authorized service provider since empirical

settings on the RTVision.t server to the authorized service provider since empirical
values are required for the optimized setting of hardware, especially the sensors.
The sensor systems delivered ex works (camera with strobe controller) always have a fixed
IP address that is identical for all sensor optics.
In addition, each sensor leaves the manufacturer with its factory calibration. Recalibration is
usually not necessary.
The strobe controller and cameras are then initialized in the RTVision.t server under Service
| Hardware state and Service | Sensor settings.
Only then can the sensor management in the RTVision.tclient be used for the productspecific assignment.

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
What happens if you work with more than 1 sensor?
If your system works with more than one physical sensor, the cameras and strobe controller
must have different IP addresses. This is necessary so that they can be controlled selectively
by the higher level controller and data transfer and evaluation is possible.
- When the strobe controller and camera have been connected to the local network, the
sensor camera is assigned a new IP address with the installed configuration software (the
software name depends on the type of camera).
- The associated strobe controller is then configured via the auxiliary program
ScLibClientQUISS\ScLibSDK\bin\Win64).

- The associated strobe controller is then configured via the auxiliary program
ScLibClientQUISS\ScLibSDK\bin\Win64).
- The Find button is used to search for the strobe controller and, if necessary, it is assigned a
new IP address.
Tip
To simplify the initialization of several sensors in RTVision.t, it is advisable to connect
and initialize just one system consisting of camera and strobe controller at a time. The
assignment of the serial number (of the camera) and sensor in RTVision.t are thus
unique.
- RTVision.t server is started.
- The basic settings are made in Service | Sensor settings in the Adjust sensor dialog.
- Delay and pulse duration are filled by default.

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
4.8.2 RGB sensor settings
Depending on the use and materials deployed, the RGB sensor provides flexibility for various
products to define the lighting with ideal light-dark contrasts.
With three color channels for blue, red and green, the RGB sensor can be adapted for every
product and its specific properties.
By combining the color channels, it can also be used for white.
The lighting is controlled by an LED light controller (strobe controller) that addresses all three
channels selectively to control the coloration.
In the RTVision.t software, the strobe controller can be configured on the server and on the
client (product-specific in this case).
Global setting

In the RTVision.t software, the strobe controller can be configured on the server and on the
client (product-specific in this case).
Global setting
On the RTVision.t server, when the strobe controller is initialized in Service | Sensor
settings, the power supply for optimum light-dark contrast is specified globally for all
products.
See details in section System variants

Selective setting of the RGB sensor per product
On the RTVision.t client, you can specify a separate color definition of the strobe controller
for each product. However, this applies only to this product and overwrites the global setting
on the server only for this product. The other products remain covered by the global definition.
In automatic mode, the strobe controller values of the RGB sensor configured for
each product in configuration mode (Client) overwrite the global settings made on the
server. This means that the RGB sensor can be optimized via the strobe controller
individually for each product by means of the client, though this can adversely affect
the testing process and possibly slow it down. For this reason, this feature of the

the testing process and possibly slow it down. For this reason, this feature of the
product-specific setting should only be used for individual products.
If the strobe controller on the client is not activated (not checked), the global settings defined
on the server for the RGB sensor are used.

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
4.8.3.1 Calibration
RTVision.t performs a visual inspection of the glue application during the application process.
This inspection is based on the images supplied by the three cameras. The image
information is shown in pixels. To make entering information as easy as possible for the user,
the software uses millimeters instead of pixels. Before being delivered, the sensors are
calibrated in the factory to establish the relationship between pixels and millimeters.
Generally, end users do not have to carry out any manual calibration.
Note - Material damage
Sensor calibration is carried out in the factory and generally does not have to be done
again by you.
Every sensor already contains its own specific calibration data on delivery. This is

again by you.
Every sensor already contains its own specific calibration data on delivery. This is
entered in the application via the sensor link.
CAUTION: Additional manual calibration overwrites the calibration values set in the
factory. Should this be unavoidable, it is imperative to consult the authorized service
provider beforehand. The manufacturer rejects all liability claims in the event of
sensors being calibrated independently.
Calibration also determines the position of the three cameras relative to each other in order to
identify the precise switching point and the overlap area.
Calibration is performed using a calibration plate with a special pattern of dots.
You will find further information on calibration in Performing a calibration .

Configuration mode description / Sensor management
4.8.3.2 Performing a calibration manually
Note - AVOID performing a manual calibration !!!
End users do not usually have to carry out any calibration manually. The calibration
data is linked to the sensor technology and calibration is carried out in the factory.
Before doing anything, contact the authorized service provider if you plan to carry
out manual calibration yourself. The manufacturer shall not be liable for any kind of
damage that occurs as a result of a manual calibration being performed
independently.
The following steps are required to perform a manual calibration:
1. In the Sensor management in Settings | Manage sensors, first select a sensor or

create a new one. Then click Settings.
2. An image of the calibration plate is required for automatic calibration. This can either be
loaded from the hard disk or recorded using the cameras.
If a new image is to be used for calibration, move the sensor to the calibration plate using the
robot, or move the calibration plate under the sensor if the glue nozzle is stationary or use the
mobile calibration device. The working distance of the sensor must be observed. The
calibration plate and sensor must also be parallel to each other (no tilting).
Then align the sensor with the calibration plate for positioning the camera. This is done by
swiveling the sensor through its fixing axis or by swiveling the calibration plate. The position is

swiveling the sensor through its fixing axis or by swiveling the calibration plate. The position is
correct when the dot outside the circle is visible in the top camera image and the dot inside
the circle is visible in the bottom camera image and these two dots are as close as possible
to the same vertical line (see screenshot below).
A good and cleanly executed calibration is a prerequisite for using the image
processing system.
Make sure to use the calibration stored on the sensor in the factory and avoid manual
calibration where possible. Do not detach the sensor link following commissioning.
You can load the calibration data for a newly replaced sensor without difficulty if a
sensor link exists by selecting Replace sensor.
To load an image from the hard disk, click Load image.

sensor link exists by selecting Replace sensor.
To load an image from the hard disk, click Load image.

Configuration mode description / Default values
4.9 Default values
Default values for new areas, references and polygons can be changed in the RTVision.t
client via the menu entry Settings | Default values . The input dialogs are the same as
the dialogs used to set the parameters for the individual areas, references and polygons.
Default values are to be understood as initial values or as a basic data record for
parameterization.
Control elements, such as basic settings, for which defining default values makes no sense,
are however locked or not included.
If any default values are changed, these changes relate only to areas, references and
polygons created from that point onwards.
For this reason, the appropriate default record is assigned to the higher-level inspection. The

polygons created from that point onwards.
For this reason, the appropriate default record is assigned to the higher-level inspection. The
inspection is opened for this purpose and the correct default record from the selection menu
is assigned under General.
Once this has been done, this corresponding data record is assigned as the initial value for
further parameterization for all new areas, references and polygons to be created.
When the area is created interactively (from the image sequence by marking with the mouse
and selecting Create area in the context menu), some parameters in the default data record
are already assigned the optimal values.
This means that some default values are already overwritten directly when the new area is

are already assigned the optimal values.
This means that some default values are already overwritten directly when the new area is
created, since the values that can be optimized directly are always set.
The contents of existing areas/references/polygons can therefore no longer be changed via a
new default link.
It is only when new ones are created in turn that a modified link would take effect in the
inspection.
Default values simplify and accelerate parameterization significantly because
they present fixed basic data, which is an ideal functionality in large projects.
Before all areas within the inspections are created, the fixed parameters for the
glue can therefore be defined, for example if error definitions are generally

glue can therefore be defined, for example if error definitions are generally
always identical for an inspection or also for the product. This error definition is
already transferred for each new area created when entered as a default.
Note that changes to default values do not impact existing areas, references and
polygons.
Creating default data records
It is recommended to create your own default data records when inspecting certain bead
types, which can work with a standardized parameterization for the entire inspection.
On the client, select Settings | Default values and create an initial data record Default_1
with Add.

Configuration mode description / Switching language
4.10 Switching language
RTVision.t can be delivered in several language versions. The basic languages are German
and English.
If you are missing languages, please contact the service partner.
The language can be switched at any time during operation, though this operation is then
password protected. It is not necessary to restart the program.
To switch to another language, select the required language version from the Language
menu.
The language is switched separately within each RTVision.t module or can also be set at a
higher level.
You will find further information on this in:
Special unlicensed specifications

Automatic mode / Result display in automatic mode
5.3 Result display in automatic mode
If the inspection system finds deviations from the set error and warning tolerances in the glue
application while inspecting a component, it outputs an NOK or warning.
In automatic mode, apart from the control elements described in the section Control elements
and displays in automatic mode the following information is also provided:

Simple statistics
The current result of the last inspection run is always displayed. This can be OK, WARN,
NOK and ERR for system errors. The latter is usually due to a hardware error.
The remaining three results, on the other hand, refer to the inspection task. Compliance
with or deviation from the set error and warning tolerances in the glue application for the
respective component is output.
Even one image recording of the image sequence with a NOK rating generates an NOK
in the quality inspection for the entire inspection run.
The number of inspected components of a product / component type is indicated with
Total, if the list entry is selected accordingly or is set to automatic.
172 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Automatic mode / Result display in automatic mode
The result display for the simple statistics can be varied in the drop-down
menu below via the list entries Automatic, Total and Product_x.
The result is assigned to the currently inspected component of the type
Product_x with the list entry Automatic. With Total, the results are
displayed as the total of all components inspected so far.
Alternatively, the display can be selected for the specific product.
The Reset button resets the display of the simple statistics to zero,
regardless of the list entry selected.
The results determined up to the reset are not deleted, rather only the display
of all products is reset to zero. This action cannot be undone. However, the
reset is performed independently of the selection.

the current reference and is likewise independent of the display mode selected for the
simple statistics.
The Statistic dialog opens by double-clicking the simple statistic. It displays the detailed
statistics data in tabular form and has to be updated manually by clicking Update.
The individual results of the various inspections are also displayed in tabular form when
the desired product is selected.

Error list
The error list shows the individual errors found during the last faulty inspection. The faulty
image recording is also replicated on the bead both on the error map and in the camera
view.
The errors found (ERROR and NOK) are described in plain text in the error information
(left). In addition, the reference values for the taught-in and actual values of the glue bead
just inspected are shown.
Clicking the errors in the list shows the associated error image, the position of the error
on the component on the error map and the reference / actual values. In the event of a

on the component on the error map and the reference / actual values. In the event of a
system error (ERR), the cause of the error is specified here. Errors that belong together
are grouped, and the errors are also allocated to the individual inspections.

with this button.

Automatic mode / Result display in automatic mode
When activated, the system automatically switches from live view (during the inspection)
to error view (with fade-in of the result) during each inspection run. If the result is OK, the
window that normally represents the error view remains empty because there is no
error. The error map is always part of each overview, regardless of the result and the
selected display mode.
If it is deactivated, a display mode of your choice can be set permanently with a mouse
click. The overview is advantageous here, as it shows the error map in large size. In the
event of a NOK, all detected warnings and errors are marked on the map with an orange
or red dot. In order to ensure a synchronous display of results, the positions must be set

or red dot. In order to ensure a synchronous display of results, the positions must be set
up correctly under Configuring the product image and bead paths .

Show / hide detailed statistics
This function displays the statistics table with the product-related result data below the
image views. An overall result is also displayed if multiple products within an application
file are created on one inspection system. All OKs, warnings, NOKs and system errors
are listed separately for each product result and overall result.
By selecting a product, the dialog described above regarding detailed statistics can be
opened. Automatic mode is not interrupted with these actions.
.
Show / hide error details
By displaying error symbols on the error map, the corresponding location on the
component is additionally marked. The typical error symbols and their meaning are
shown under Overview of error icons

Switching the camera view
The display type of the camera images is preset here. It is possible to switch between
the single camera views and the round view, i.e. the 360° view. The sampling point can
be observed in both views in live mode. The images transferred from the sensor
cameras are shown next to the error map. The live images can be seen there during an
inspection run. The five sampling points are also shown in the camera image in which
the glue bead can be seen. The number of sampling points can be recognized by the
number of green lines - five is the default setting. The blue direction indicates the search
direction for the component-glue-component transitions. The yellow line stands for the

direction for the component-glue-component transitions. The yellow line stands for the
width calculation at this point, incl. display of the position detection (important in curves).

Automatic mode / Communication dialog
5.5 Communication dialog
Choosing View | Communication opens the communications dialog between the RTVision.t
(server) and the controls. It can be used to check whether the bus connection is active. In the
dialog shown below, the connection to the higher-level controller has been correctly
established. The control lamp is active (green), the system indicates active communication.
The All bytes tab shows the inputs and outputs as numerical values. The display can be
toggled between the following types:
· Binary: binary display, e.g. "00000100"
· Decimal: decimal display, e.g. "005"
· Hexadecimal: hexadecimal display, e.g. "05"
Operating manual RTVision.t 185
V. 4.8

Automatic mode / Communication dialog
The second tab "Base I/O" shows the current states of the inputs and outputs of the basic
interface within the context of the RTVision.tlog.
The third tab, Repair I/O, is only visible if the repair function in the software is activated and
the corresponding signal inputs / outputs are defined. The dialog shows the inputs and
outputs required for the repair.

Automatic mode / Logbook, history and statistics
5.6 Logbook, history and statistics
The RTVision.t (server) module allows you to display three types of log from the View menu.
View | Display logbook
Opens the logbook on the server with all system and diagnostics messages that were
recorded (logon/logoff, switch language, communication, application transfer).
To see the logbook in client configuration mode, likewise select View | Display logbook.
Control elements in the logbook
Clear all
Clears the display of all logbook entries except for the last current entry. The display allows up
to 1000 entries; if the display is deleted, the content remains stored in the logbook file, only
the display has been cleaned.
Group

to 1000 entries; if the display is deleted, the content remains stored in the logbook file, only
the display has been cleaned.
Group
Selects the logbook entries that are to be displayed according to the categories All groups,
System, Inspection or Communication.
Min. Priority
Selects the priority level for displaying the logbook entries. Each level is displayed with
"green", while normal messages are filtered with "yellow", which are to be assigned to this
and the red level. With "red", only messages marked in red that show errors are displayed.
188 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Automatic mode / Logbook, history and statistics
Last messages
Is checked by default and limits the focus to the most recent logbook entry. This is particularly
beneficial in automatic mode. You can deselect this button if you want to browse older
logbook entries.
Entries already deleted in the display are still in the log file and can be viewed there.
Display signals
This function outputs additional messages that relate to the signal communication between
the higher-level system and RTVision.t . Signals such as "Start inspection" and "Stop
inspection" are logged here for each call associated with a signal transfer. This fills the log file
within a very short time and should therefore only be activated during commissioning.
Deactivation is recommended in normal production mode.
Close

within a very short time and should therefore only be activated during commissioning.
Deactivation is recommended in normal production mode.
Close
Closes the logbook.
View | Display history
Opens the history in which the product-specific results are output. The results OK / NOK /
ERR and WARN are listed together with the product name, date and time stamp, operation
mode and acquisition status.
The "Acquire state" column is only filled if the storage status of an image sequence is also
captured when it is recorded. Regardless of the result, only the completeness of the saved
image sequence is displayed in the last column "Acquire state." Complete stands for the
complete closure of the saved image sequence. An error occurs, for example, when there is

complete closure of the saved image sequence. An error occurs, for example, when there is
insufficient memory or other reasons exist for a "component not inspected". By doubleclicking the respective line, the corresponding detailed information can be called up in the
viewer that opens.

Reset

statistics are automatically updated with each inspection run.

Use Settings to open the dialog of the same name, in which you can specify a time window

Use Settings to open the dialog of the same name, in which you can specify a time window
with total duration in the past via the Statistic view. Time window options include All or 1
day(s) or week(s) or month(s). 1 day means the current day (today), 2 days means, for
example, today and yesterday.
This not only defines the display of results in the detailed statistics dialog, but also influences
the view of the simple (automatically updating) statistics in the server dialog (automatic
mode). Please note that the settings for the statistic view and the trend indicator are
independent of each other.

statistics. It is the starting point for recalculating the display values.

Automatic mode / Recording image sequences
5.7 Recording image sequences
To be able to parameterize the system, you must start by recording image sequences. This
is done in automatic mode.
To make the configuration possible, the products must be created beforehand.
1. In automatic mode in the menu bar first click Settings | Record image sequence.

Automatic mode will not be exited while the recorded sequences are being
configured.
2. Authorize yourself by entering a password in the Login dialog.
3. Select the relevant product in the Record images window.
4. Now click the Configure image recording button or double-click the relevant product.
5. Enter the number of required image sequence recordings directly in the numerical field in
the Configure image recording window or increase the number using the arrow buttons.
Click OK to confirm.
Long glue beads are automatically divided into multiple image sequences.
Multiple passes are required in this case. In each of these passes, the inspection
system attempts to record as many inspections as possible. At a minimum,

system attempts to record as many inspections as possible. At a minimum,
however, one inspection per image sequence will be recorded. This means that
the product and number of inspections must already be known and created for
the configuration.
The record counter is only reset when all the inspections selected in the Record images
dialog have been recorded.
6. Optionally, you can select the inspections to be recorded. To do this, click the Select
individual inspections button.

Automatic mode / Saving result data
5.8 Saving result data
Saving the result data on the server makes it possible to save not just error data but all the
data recorded by the system during a component inspection to an individual XML file (one
XML file is created per component). This server setting applies globally.
Note - Required for detailed view in the viewer
This setting is mandatory if the viewer is to be used fully to analyze past results. This
default setting is required for the detailed view in the viewer to provide the necessary
data.
Results data can also be changed and saved for each product, with the frequency indicating
which component result (of a product) will be saved.

Automatic mode / User management
5.9 User management
The user manager can be used to create users globally who are assigned a user level.
Each user level is defined with fixed access rights by the system, distinguishing between
"Administrator", "User" and "Disabled".
Access rights are not assigned individually to the levels, but are fixed.
Choose Disabled to block the user. The advantage of this is that the user data is retained, but
the user has no rights.
Access can thus be activated and deactivated for a short time.
Full access exists for the administrator level, while the user is often restricted with respect to
global setting variants.
Calling up the user manager
To launch the user manager, select Settings | Manage users in the menu bar. To be able to

global setting variants.

use the user manager, you must have administrator rights in RTVision.t .

The new user does not have a password yet. You can rename a user and change the user
level by clicking the Adjust button.

To delete users, first select the required user(s) in the list of users. Then click Delete.
Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes. The currently logged in user cannot be
deleted and neither can the administrator, even after acknowledging the confirmation prompt.

Automatic mode / Switching language
5.10 Switching language
RTVision.t can be delivered in several language versions. The basic languages are German
and English.
If you are missing languages, please contact the service partner. Integration of additional
languages is possible, but must be ordered from the manufacturer as a language package.
The languages can be changed at any time during operation, but the switching feature may
be password protected. It is not necessary to restart the program.
To switch to another language, select the required language version from the Language
menu.
The language is switched separately within each RTVision.t module or can also be set at a
higher level.
If the associated optional unlicensed specification is set up, the system can be preset so that

higher level.
If the associated optional unlicensed specification is set up, the system can be preset so that
all other modules operate in line with the language setting of the first module opened. The
individual language switch per module is no longer necessary. In addition to the display of the
interface in the local language, the names are assigned automatically when new products,
inspections, areas, references and polygons are created in the set language.
You will find further information on this in:
Special unlicensed specifications

6 Results evaluation
6.1 The viewer
The RTVision.t system logs all relevant result data in the background and saves it in special
xml files on the server for further processing. This data can be loaded and displayed via these
result files. The viewer is a useful enhancement to the system for presenting result data and
its error evaluation in graphical form.
When a component inspection has been completed, select Display history in the View main
menu. The history opens. Double-click a row to open the viewer.
The date, time, product name and result status are shown in the history. If the list is displayed
via the server, double-clicking a line opens the viewer with the selected individual result of the
product.

Chapter 7

Communication / General
7 Communication
7.1 General
The inspection system RTVision.t must be able to communicate with the robot controller
(hereinafter called the "controller") for the purpose of exchanging various signals and data.
The signals are used, for example, to transmit inspection results, start / stop events or
general system messages (see below for details); the image processing system is always
the secondary system in this case.
Communication with the controller takes place via fieldbus systems (Profibus, DeviceNet,
CANopen) or Industrial Ethernet systems (Profinet, EtherNet/IP, EtherCAT).
The basic interface can be adapted in terms of its communication with existing glue
controllers and additional signals exchanged by extending the interface.

controllers and additional signals exchanged by extending the interface.
Tip - Joint definition of interface
Prior to installing the RTVision.t system, consult with manufacturer in order to define
details of the data transfer jointly in advance.
The sequence of communication from start to finish for RTVision.t is as follows:

Communication / Basic interface
7.2.1 Incoming signals
Product ON / OFF (0.0)
A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of a product, a change from 1 to 0 ends the current
product.
Inspection ON / OFF (0.1)
A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of the inspection period, a change from 1 to 0 ends
the current inspection area. Signal changes in this bit will be ignored if no product is selected
(i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0).
Change inspection (0.2)
Each signal edge (change from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0) during an inspection period (i.e. "Product
ON / OFF" is 1 and "Inspection ON / OFF" is 1) causes an uninterruptible change to the next
inspection. Signal edges outside of inspection periods (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0 or
"Inspection ON / OFF" is 0) will be ignored.

inspection. Signal edges outside of inspection periods (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0 or
"Inspection ON / OFF" is 0) will be ignored.
This function is required if inspection parameters, such as the exposure settings for the
sensor, are to be changed without interruption during application of the glue or sealant.
Product ID (1.0 - 1.7)
The number to select the product (component) is an 8Bit unsigned integer. The number is
read when a product is selected (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" from 0 to 1).
Speed (2.0 - 3.7)
The system receives the speed of the robot as an unsigned integer via bits 2.0 to 3.7. A 0
(zero) corresponds to 0 mm/s and the value 65,535 ((2^16) - 1) corresponds to the maximum
speed that can be transmitted. This can be configured in mm/s. The transmitted data must

speed that can be transmitted. This can be configured in mm/s. The transmitted data must
use the same multiplier that has also been stored in the system. Configuration takes place in
the file "RTVision_t.ini". This file may only be processed explicitly by the authorized
service provider. The assignment is linear.
Operating manual RTVision.t 213
V. 4.8

Communication / Basic interface
7.2.2 Outgoing signals
Mirrored signals
The mirrored signals indicate which input signals are currently applied to the system.
However, delays can occur between the input signal (e.g. input: Product ON / OFF à 1) and
the mirror (e.g. output: Product ON / OFF à 1). These delays are caused by tasks the system
has to perform first in order to achieve the required status.
The following signals are mirrored:
Product ON / OFF (0.0)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Product ON / OFF".
Inspection ON / OFF (0.1)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Inspection ON / OFF".
Change inspection (0.2)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Change inspection".
Product ID (1.0 - 1.7)
Mirrors the incoming product ID. The product ID is read in at the moment the product is

Product ID (1.0 - 1.7)
Mirrors the incoming product ID. The product ID is read in at the moment the product is
selected (Product ON / OFF à 1).
214 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

Communication / Basic interface
Status messages
The status messages provide information on the actual, current system status. There can
also be delays between the input signal and the change in status with these signals due to
tasks the system needs to perform.
The following states can be reported:
System ON / OFF (2.0)
Is set to 1 when the vision system is started.
Automatic ON / OFF (2.1)
Is set to 1 when automatic mode is started.
Product selectable (2.2)
Is set to 1 when the system is expecting a product to be selected.
Inspection selectable (2.3)
Is set to 1 when an inspection can be selected.
Inspection changeable (2.4)
Is set to 1 if an inspection can be changed.
Result available (2.5)
Is set to 1 if a product was ended and therefore a result is available (OK, WARN, NOK, ERR).

Result available (2.5)
Is set to 1 if a product was ended and therefore a result is available (OK, WARN, NOK, ERR).
Hardware ready (2.6)
The sensor, camera and strobe controller are checked by the system when the program is
started, when the product is selected and also when the sensor is checked manually to
determine if it is correct.
Signal is set to 1 if the hardware status is OK, to zero if one or more components (also cable
connection) is/are faulty.
Result messages:
The system uses the result messages to report the result of the most recent run.
The following results can be reported:
Product result OK / WARN / NOK / ERR (3.0 – 3.3)
If the signal "Result available" is set to 1, the system transmits the corresponding result with
these bits.
Operating manual RTVision.t 215
V. 4.8

Communication / Basic interface
7.2.3 Programming the controller
Selecting a product
A product may only be selected by the controller if the following signals are set:
Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals )

2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 1
Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals )

0.0 "Product ON / OFF" to 0
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0
Sequence on the controller side
1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.
2. Check whether the outgoing signals from the controller (see above) are set correctly.
3. Send product number to RTVision.t .
4. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 1
When the product has been selected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
1.0-1.7 "Product ID" correspond to the bits of the product number
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1
If there is RTVision.t no product with the transmitted product number, the signal
"Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 at the time when "Product ON / OFF" is
mirrored.

Deselecting a product
A product may only be deselected by the controller if the following signals are set:
Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals )

2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1

0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0
Sequence on the controller side
1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.
2. Check whether the outgoing signals from the controller (see above) are set correctly.
3. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 0
When the product has been deselected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
0.0 "Product ON / OFF" to 0
2.2 "Product selectable" to 1
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0
2.5 "Result available" to 1
Canceling a product
Changing the signal "Product ON / OFF" from 1 to 0 during an inspection run cancels the
product inspection, i.e. the inspection currently in progress is stopped. A system error (ERR)
will be returned in this case.

8 Special specifications – optional
RTVision.t offers additional options and functionalities on the basis of the specifications
required in projects.
These are not available by default and in some cases have to be enabled via license. In
addition, they usually require configuration adjustments on the customer side.
For queries regarding activation and necessary configuration changes, please contact the
service partner who supports you.
The following extensions are possible in principle, but are not included in the installation by
default:
· Target check: Configuration and workflow

· Defining the repair interface (licensed option) and the repair area

· Interface extension - Extended communication

· Communicating with the glue controller

· Interface extension - Extended communication

· Communicating with the glue controller

· Transfer of seam numbers to inspections

· Provision of data for MQTT processing

· Unlicensed specifications

· Ghost mode

· Optional database usage with PostgreSQL

Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow
8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow
For the target check, the complete system and its individual components (from the robot
gripper arm through the dispenser with nozzle to the sensor) are referenced against each
other, i.e. an image recording of the reference target is optimized in configuration mode
(Client) and saved as a reference value in the product or application.
The application configured in this way with a specific product is transmitted to the server,
where it is available for a repeat target check after a predefined product ID is retrieved.
It checks the extent to which the current system status concerning imaging inspection

It checks the extent to which the current system status concerning imaging inspection
deviates from a saved target status (reference). This is done using a permanently mounted
reference target, which is approached when called.
One possible application would be, for example, saving a cyclic call of the target check in the
PLC so that the reference and actual position of the system with respect to the reference
target can be determined.
Furthermore, the test program can also be started after every mechanical replacement of a
component (e.g. gripper, robot, sensor, nozzle, etc.) involving changes within the system
and, if the result is NOK, the cause of the error can be determined and the magnitude of the
error can be checked with regard to its relevance.

and, if the result is NOK, the cause of the error can be determined and the magnitude of the
error can be checked with regard to its relevance.
The settings made are specific for each station, as they reference the robot, sensor, gripper
arm and dispenser with nozzle together as an (overall) system.
Note
This test program is an optional function of the RTVision.t system and can be
enabled after installation. Significant effort is required for the configuration and has to
be clarified in advance before commissioning.
The functional checkbox in the RTVision.t client is called Compare sensor at product
level.
If necessary, contact the authorized service partner.
Note: Configuration of a reference is NOT the same as sensor calibration. The latter

level.
If necessary, contact the authorized service partner.
Note: Configuration of a reference is NOT the same as sensor calibration. The latter
is carried out in-house by the manufacturer and is permanently saved on the sensor
upon delivery.
Repeat calibration (manual) via RTVision.t is generally not required and is not
intended. The manufacturer accepts no liability for any damage resulting from
independently performed manual calibration of the sensor.
For configuration of the target check, paths must be programmed (by the plant
manufacturer) and at the same time settings made in the RTVision.tsystem.
For configuration of the target check, a reference image of the reference target is saved for

For configuration of the target check, a reference image of the reference target is saved for
the current sensor. In the target checks that are then performed automatically, this reference
is checked for current deviations of the nozzle position, which can be set in pixels. For better
orientation, the conversion of the pixel data into millimeters is displayed in an information field,
depending on the calibration of the sensor used.

Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow
Procedure - robot controller
· The reference target is mounted as close as possible to "Home" and must be
unchangeable for repeated approach of the industrial robot.
· The robot controller must be programmed so that when a predefined product ID is called,
the center of the nozzle is directed to the center of the reference target, with the nozzle
positioned perpendicular to the plate.
· The working distance must be maintained with a deviation of +/- 1 mm and the center
points must be exactly above each other.
· The robot arm rotates the sensor head until the three cameras display an optimal image
of the reference target.
· Note this in the RTVision.t client in Settings | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings

of the reference target.
· Note this in the RTVision.t client in Settings | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings
(see below). It is considered optimal if all three cameras find and display the circle points
on top of each other.
When the optimal position is reached, this position must be saved as a fixed target position
before the reference target in the industrial robot.
Note - Observe in the robot controller!
The test program created is called in the RTVision.t server. The predefined product
ID is called with the saved inspection. For communication, the following applies:
product ON, inspection ON, a short wait time must be observed (e.g. <100 ms before
the reference target), the signal for inspection OFF, product OFF then follows. The

the reference target), the signal for inspection OFF, product OFF then follows. The
robot can then leave the position before the reference target.
The result OK / NOK is output in automatic mode (server).
For the initial configuration described here, additional settings in the RTVision.tclient are
required.
Procedure - settings in the RTVision.tclient in two steps

and

shown vertically below each other, the better the reference image settings.
In most cases, it is sufficient to optimize the point search by means of the
brightness threshold so that the default values for the point filtering can be retained.

Result interpretation - check errors!
If, for example, deviations with a tolerance of less than 4 px (~1 mm) are acceptable, the
server result is OK for the target check.
If the deviation of the calculated nozzle position is greater than 4 px, the server result after
completion of the inspection would be NOK.
This is generally due to inaccuracy in one of the system components and should be checked
before the start of production.
However, before making corrections to the hardware components, the error message
from the target check should be verified with respect to the severity of the error and
its relevance.

from the target check should be verified with respect to the severity of the error and
its relevance.
The error message NOK is output on the server side with no additional information. Here, the
RTVision.tclient offers the option of an analysis or reconfiguration for the target check.
· If the result is NOK on the server side, load the automatically saved error image
sequence by clicking the Load error image button.
· By clicking Load reference image, the image originally saved with the reference
configuration can be loaded and viewed again.
· By clicking the Compare sensor button you can compare the set parameters with the
current reference image and analyze the accurate px result at a glance.

Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)
8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)
The repair function requires extended signal exchange between the inspection system
RTVision.t and the systems involved in the process, such as glue controller, robot controller
and higher level PLC. By way of illustration, these systems should be collectively treated as a
higher-level controller.
Like the inspection itself, the repair is performed on the basis of the product. In addition to
supplying the product result, the inspection system signals to the higher-level controller
whether a part that returned NOK can be repaired. The higher-level controller repeats the
component program (robot path) if repair is required. It also signals to the inspection system

component program (robot path) if repair is required. It also signals to the inspection system
that the most recently inspected component is to be repaired. The inspection system then
provides information about the points on the bead where the applicator nozzle needs to be
opened for the purpose of reworking the bead; this information is provided for the repair run
as an output signal.
Signals
Bit offset Signal output Signal input
0 Product reparable Repair product
1 Close nozzle
Note
The additional repair signals happen in the same time frame as the signals of the
basic interface PV2.
Product reparable (0)
Whether a repair is possible is output synchronously with the output signal "Result available"

basic interface PV2.
Product reparable (0)
Whether a repair is possible is output synchronously with the output signal "Result available"
after the first inspection. The signal is reset when the next product is selected. The previous
component can no longer be repaired if no repair is performed with the next product selected.
Repair product (0)
If, when a product is selected, the signal is set and the previous inspection detected a
reparable component, the output signal "Close nozzle" can be used for the repair. In addition,
the product ID for the repair inspection must not change compared with the first inspection.
Close nozzle (1)

Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)
8.2.1 Defining the repair area
Note
The repair function is an optional enhancement of RTVision.t and must be enabled
separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level
controller must also be extended. The associated Repair mode tab is only visible if
the repair function is enabled.
Note
The repair is only possible if the function is active for the application, product,
inspection and area. This allows the repair to be easily controlled from the
superordinate level. The repair settings for the area properties impose further
restrictions in terms of repairability.
In special cases, a component that returns a NOK result can be upgraded to OK through

restrictions in terms of repairability.
In special cases, a component that returns a NOK result can be upgraded to OK through
subsequent automatic reworking (repair) and repeat inspection. However, this option is only
available if the error can be fixed by reapplying glue. For example, gaps in a glue bead can be
touched up but position errors and violations of the maximum glue width cannot be repaired.
The system remembers the position of the faults and can rework them in a second run.

following settings can also be made:

Special specifications – optional / Interface extension
8.3 Interface extension
The basic interface comprises 4 standard bytes in each case and can be extended through
the addition of further bytes to create a customized interface.
Some options are provided as an example here, which have been implemented to varying
degrees to date together with customers.
Note - special specifications
This involves the implementation of special specifications, which are to be defined
between the operator and the manufacturer BEFORE commissioning.
Depending on the request, the interface can only be extended in consultation with
development and the customer specification; it usually involves the manufacturer
adapting the application, rather than arbitrary adjustments on site by the service
department.

adapting the application, rather than arbitrary adjustments on site by the service
department.
Robot path (only on request):
The standard RTVision.t system is a time-based inspection system, whose basic unit is
generally based on [ms]. A path-based approach can be followed here by using the robot
coordinates provided and communication via n bytes. Here too: This special specification
must be defined and programmed in advance; it cannot be integrated as standard.
Inspection result query:
After a component (product) has been inspected, the results of the individual inspections can
be queried (OK, WARN, NOK). After activating the functionality, communication must be
configured via the extended interface using n bytes (usually 5). Once this is done, each

configured via the extended interface using n bytes (usually 5). Once this is done, each
inspection can be assigned its own inspection ID in configuration mode. The inspection result
query can only be issued when the product result is available, it is recognized as valid and the
query is then executed according to the inspection ID.
Here too: This special specification must be defined and configured in advance; it cannot be
integrated as standard.
Take over seam numbers:
The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, in which transmitted or stored
seam numbers are taken over and processed internally by the image processing system.
Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized
service partner. See Take over seam numbers

Transferring serial numbers:
The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, which include transmitted serial
numbers in the result data.
Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized
service partner.
The data type (ASCII, hexadecimal or integers) must be defined, while the start byte for the
serial number and the maximum length of the serial number must likewise be specified.

Special specifications – optional / Communicating with the glue controller
8.4 Communicating with the glue controller
RTVision.t communicates with the glue controller used via the extended interface link.
Depending on the configuration of the corresponding software program, signals can be
exchanged and, if necessary, communicated with the aid of color-coded signaling.
This option has to be specified in advance however and cannot be configured subsequently
on the installed RTVision.t system.
By default, the RTVision.t is the higher-level communication partner in this case.
This is visualized on the software interface on the server screen with an appropriately labeled
button.
This button allows a direct switch from the RTVision.t server to the glue controller software

button.
This button allows a direct switch from the RTVision.t server to the glue controller software
and indicates (depending on the glue controller software used) basic messages by a change
of color.
Button Meaning Action

controller communication will be established
at startup.

Orange There is a warning from the glue Verification of the data by the glue
controller controller is advisable.
Flashing red An error occurred on the part of the Switch immediately to the glue
glue controller controller software to inspect the
source of the error.

Special specifications – optional / Data transfer via MQTT
8.6 Data transfer via MQTT
The RTVision.t system allows a number of files to be provided for loading on / collection by
the customer's MQTT system thanks to its comprehensive interface extension.
The program provided separately for this purpose monitors the data generated by RTVision.t
at directory level, extracts the required information and sends it to the predefined server.
This extended data storage allows the data requested by the customer to be made available
for MQTT (using the MQ Telemetry Transport) via a simple dialog.
As an external auxiliary tool, this product is reserved for the service department and is
delivered with German and English interface only.

As an external auxiliary tool, this product is reserved for the service department and is
delivered with German and English interface only.
Additional programming effort is likewise needed for RTVision.t to provide the data, which
means that a standard procedure is not used here.
Hence, the previous definition of how the data transfer works has to be defined together in
advance.
You will find further information on this in:
Specific interface extension as a special specification

Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications
8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications
The specifications outlined here are unlicensed and can be activated via the authorized
service partner.
Note that provision or transfer of data may nonetheless also be required on the part of the
operator to ensure successful use of the option.
Tip - Clarify the desired configuration together in advance
Prior to commissioning therefore, discuss your desired configuration with the
unlicensed specifications.
Ghost mode
Ghost mode is an important tool for local commissioning staff because important system
functions, particularly for communication and hardware, can be tested without products
needing to be parameterized fully. Results are output that show the correct system

needing to be parameterized fully. Results are output that show the correct system
functionality, though they are not recorded in the statistics data. This means that the image
processing system has no impact on the production process during ghost mode. The
applicable icon in this case is shown in large form in automatic mode (server) when the
mode is active. See Ghost mode

Optional database usage
The data can be transferred optionally from the directory system to the PostgreSQL
database, which is supplied on request.
Its use is not necessary for single systems. This is also the case with networked systems,
but if there is a requirement for database storage then this option is possible from V.4.8.
See Optional database usage

Electronic access control
RTVision.t can verify and accept access authorization via electronic key systems (e.g.
Euchner key).
No entry is needed in the user management in this case.
However, if access to the software is required independently of an Electronic Key System
(EKS) for security reasons (for example, in the case of a deputizing arrangement), the
employee should be managed in the user management with their access authorization and
password assignment as a precaution, as otherwise there is no possibility to access
RTVision.t without the Electronic Key System.
Explorer button (server)
On the right of the toolbar at the top, an additional button can be displayed in the server
module if required, which can be used to open Windows Explorer with password protection.

module if required, which can be used to open Windows Explorer with password protection.
This is a good way to open Explorer specifically without a keyboard at touch-operated
workstations.

8.7.1 Ghost mode
Ghost mode plays an important role in the course of on-site commissioning.
The system functions are tested with regard to
· Communication between the glue controller and RTVision.t,
· Hardware signals,
· Parameter test for target check (sensor comparison).
Ghost mode runs without being linked to the other production processes and can be activated
without a product already having been created and taught in.
The result can be ERROR or OK. If the status of communication and hardware is OK, an OK
is always displayed as a result in the RTVision.t server.
This OK does not refer to a product / component. Existing statistics data is therefore not

is always displayed as a result in the RTVision.t server.
This OK does not refer to a product / component. Existing statistics data is therefore not
influenced. All component testing is bypassed in Ghost mode and a result of OK is output,
which is not recorded via the statistics.
The extent to which the three tested components are faulty, missing or functioning perfectly in
terms of their signaling is indicated by a red or green status signal in the Commissioning
dialog. The status is displayed here.
In addition, it is possible to output these statuses with a date and time stamp using a pdf short
report. These reports are saved under Report | Service.
With the result OK, all technologies including the RTVision.tsystem are ready to run and

report. These reports are saved under Report | Service.
With the result OK, all technologies including the RTVision.tsystem are ready to run and
active at the end of commissioning – a proven means, for example, to bypass the image
processing inspection system temporarily in the course of performance tests, without
switching it off.
Note – Installation of hardware complete – Verification in ghost mode
Assuming that the RTVision.t system is fully installed and connected with its
hardware, both the RTVision.t server and client modules should be started.
At this point, no product (not even sensor comparison/target check) need have been
created as yet. Lack of parameterization does not lead to an error in ghost mode on
the server.

Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications
8.7.2 Optional database usage
RTVision.t generally uses data storage solely as a file system structure.
Now for the first time, Version 4.8 also offers a free option for transferring and storing data in
a database via the file system.
The database storage feature is useful in the case of network integration of multiple systems.
The database can be filled with all actual and target data from the image processing software,
but image sequences cannot be stored there.
In the case of single systems, the database does not offer any real advantage, rather you can
work purely via the directory storage as usual.
